subject: Mathematics
book: Class 12 - Mathematics - Exemplar Book by NCERT
solution_text: Let {tex}I = \int {\frac{{2x - 1}}{{2x + 3}}dx = \int {\frac{{2x + 3 - 3 - 1}}{{2x + 3}}} dx} {/tex} {tex}= \int {1dx - 4\int {\frac{1}{{2x + 3}}dx = x - \int {\frac{4}{{2\left( {x + \frac{3}{2}} \right)}}dx} } } {/tex} {tex}= x - 2\log \left| {\left( {x + \frac{3}{2}} \right)} \right|+C' = x - 2\log \left| {\left( {\frac{{2x + 3}}{2}} \right)} \right| + C'{/tex} {tex} = x - 2\log \left| {2x + 3} \right| + 2\log 2 + C'\left[ {\because \log \frac{m}{n} = \log m - \log n} \right]{/tex} {tex} = x - \log \left| {{{\left( {2x + 3} \right)}^2}} \right| + C\,\,\left[ {\because C' = 2\log 2 + C} \right]{/tex}
solution_html:
Let {tex}I = \int {\frac{{2x - 1}}{{2x + 3}}dx = \int {\frac{{2x + 3 - 3 - 1}}{{2x + 3}}} dx} {/tex}

{tex}= \int {1dx - 4\int {\frac{1}{{2x + 3}}dx = x - \int {\frac{4}{{2\left( {x + \frac{3}{2}} \right)}}dx} } } {/tex}

{tex}= x - 2\log \left| {\left( {x + \frac{3}{2}} \right)} \right|+C' = x - 2\log \left| {\left( {\frac{{2x + 3}}{2}} \right)} \right| + C'{/tex}

{tex} = x - 2\log \left| {2x + 3} \right| + 2\log 2 + C'\left[ {\because \log \frac{m}{n} = \log m - \log n} \right]{/tex}

{tex} = x - \log \left| {{{\left( {2x + 3} \right)}^2}} \right| + C\,\,\left[ {\because C' = 2\log 2 + C} \right]{/tex}

question_text: Evaluate {tex}\int {\frac{{{e^{6\log x}} - {e^{5\log x}}}}{{{e^{4\log x}} - {e^{3\log x}}}}} dx{/tex}
question_html: Evaluate {tex}\int {\frac{{{e^{6\log x}} - {e^{5\log x}}}}{{{e^{4\log x}} - {e^{3\log x}}}}} dx{/tex}
solution_text: Let, {tex}I = \int {\frac{{{e^{6\log x}} - {e^{5\log x}}}}{{{e^{4\log x}} - {e^{3\log x}}}}} dx{/tex} {tex}= \int {\left( {\frac{{{e^{\log {x^6}}} - {e^{\log {x^5}}}}}{{{e^{\log {x^4}}} - {e^{\log {x^3}}}}}} \right)} dx\,\,\left[ {\because a\log b = \log {b^a}} \right]{/tex} {tex}= \int {\left( {\frac{{{x^6} - {x^5}}}{{{x^{\mathbf{4}}} - {x^3}}}} \right)} dx\,\,\left[ {\because {e^{\log x}} = x} \right]{/tex} {tex}= \int {\left( {\frac{{{x^3} - {x^2}}}{{x - 1}}} \right)} dx = \int {\frac{{{x^2}\left( {x - 1} \right)}}{{x - 1}}} dx{/tex} {tex} = \int {{x^2}dx = \frac{{{x^3}}}{3} + C} {/tex}
solution_html:
Let, {tex}I = \int {\frac{{{e^{6\log x}} - {e^{5\log x}}}}{{{e^{4\log x}} - {e^{3\log x}}}}} dx{/tex}
{tex}= \int {\left( {\frac{{{e^{\log {x^6}}} - {e^{\log {x^5}}}}}{{{e^{\log {x^4}}} - {e^{\log {x^3}}}}}} \right)} dx\,\,\left[ {\because a\log b = \log {b^a}} \right]{/tex}
{tex}= \int {\left( {\frac{{{x^6} - {x^5}}}{{{x^{\mathbf{4}}} - {x^3}}}} \right)} dx\,\,\left[ {\because {e^{\log x}} = x} \right]{/tex}
{tex}= \int {\left( {\frac{{{x^3} - {x^2}}}{{x - 1}}} \right)} dx = \int {\frac{{{x^2}\left( {x - 1} \right)}}{{x - 1}}} dx{/tex}
{tex} = \int {{x^2}dx = \frac{{{x^3}}}{3} + C} {/tex}

solution_text: Given integral is: {tex}\int \frac{d x}{1+\cos x}{/tex} = {tex}\int \frac{(1-\cos x)}{(1+\cos x)(1-\cos x)} d x{/tex} = {tex}\int \frac{1-\cos x}{1-\cos ^{2} x} d x{/tex} = {tex}\int \frac{1-\cos x}{\sin ^{2} x} d x{/tex} = {tex}\int\left(cosec ^{2} x-cosec x \cot x\right) d x{/tex} = - cot x + cosec x + C
solution_html:
Given integral is: {tex}\int \frac{d x}{1+\cos x}{/tex} 
= {tex}\int \frac{(1-\cos x)}{(1+\cos x)(1-\cos x)} d x{/tex} 
= {tex}\int \frac{1-\cos x}{1-\cos ^{2} x} d x{/tex} 
= {tex}\int \frac{1-\cos x}{\sin ^{2} x} d x{/tex}
= {tex}\int\left(cosec ^{2} x-cosec x \cot x\right) d x{/tex} 
= - cot x + cosec x + C

question_text: Evaluate {tex}\int \frac{\sin x+\cos x}{\sqrt{1+\sin 2 x}} d x{/tex}
question_html: Evaluate {tex}\int \frac{\sin x+\cos x}{\sqrt{1+\sin 2 x}} d x{/tex}
solution_text: Given integral is: {tex}\int \frac{\sin x+\cos x}{\sqrt{1+\sin 2 x}} d x{/tex} = {tex}\int {\frac{{\sin \,x + \cos \,x}}{{\sqrt {{{\sin }^2} + {{\cos }^2}x + 2\sin x\cos x} }}dx} {/tex} = {tex}\int \frac{\sin x+\cos x}{\sqrt{(\sin x+\cos x)^{2}}} d x{/tex} = {tex}\int \frac{(\sin x+\cos x)}{(\sin x+\cos x)}d x{/tex} = {tex}\int 1 d x{/tex} = x + C
solution_html:
Given integral is: {tex}\int \frac{\sin x+\cos x}{\sqrt{1+\sin 2 x}} d x{/tex} 
= {tex}\int {\frac{{\sin \,x + \cos \,x}}{{\sqrt {{{\sin }^2} + {{\cos }^2}x + 2\sin x\cos x} }}dx} {/tex}
= {tex}\int \frac{\sin x+\cos x}{\sqrt{(\sin x+\cos x)^{2}}} d x{/tex}
= {tex}\int \frac{(\sin x+\cos x)}{(\sin x+\cos x)}d x{/tex} 
= {tex}\int 1 d x{/tex} 
= x + C

solution_text: Let {tex}I = \int {\frac{x}{{\sqrt x + 1}}dx} {/tex} Put {tex}\sqrt x = t \Rightarrow \frac{1}{{2\sqrt x }}dx = dt{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow dx = 2\sqrt x dt{/tex} {tex}\therefore I = 2\int \left( {\frac{{x\sqrt x }}{{t + 1}}} \right)dt {/tex} {tex}= 2\int \frac{{{t^2}.t}}{{t + 1}}dt = 2\int \frac{{{t^3}}}{{t + 1}}dt{/tex} {tex}= 2\int \frac{{{t^3} + 1 - 1}}{{t + 1}}dt {/tex} {tex}= 2\int\frac{t^3+1}{t+1} dt- 2\int {\frac{1}{{t + 1}}dt} {/tex} {tex}= 2\int {\frac{{\left( {t + 1} \right)\left( {{t^2} - t + 1} \right)}}{{t + 1}}dt - 2\int {\frac{1}{{t + 1}}dt} }{/tex} {tex}= 2\int {\left( {{t^2} - t + 1} \right)dt - 2\int {\frac{1}{{t + 1}}d} t} {/tex} {tex}= 2\left[ {\frac{{{t^3}}}{3} - \frac{{{t^2}}}{2} + t - \log \left| {\left( {t + 1} \right)} \right|} \right] + C{/tex} {tex}= 2\left[ {\frac{{x\sqrt x }}{2} - \frac{x}{2} + \sqrt x - \log \left| {\sqrt x + 1} \right|} \right] + C{/tex}
solution_html:
Let {tex}I = \int {\frac{x}{{\sqrt x + 1}}dx} {/tex}

Put {tex}\sqrt x = t \Rightarrow \frac{1}{{2\sqrt x }}dx = dt{/tex}

{tex}\Rightarrow dx = 2\sqrt x dt{/tex}

{tex}\therefore I = 2\int \left( {\frac{{x\sqrt x }}{{t + 1}}} \right)dt {/tex} {tex}= 2\int \frac{{{t^2}.t}}{{t + 1}}dt = 2\int \frac{{{t^3}}}{{t + 1}}dt{/tex} {tex}= 2\int \frac{{{t^3} + 1 - 1}}{{t + 1}}dt {/tex} {tex}= 2\int\frac{t^3+1}{t+1} dt- 2\int {\frac{1}{{t + 1}}dt} {/tex} {tex}= 2\int {\frac{{\left( {t + 1} \right)\left( {{t^2} - t + 1} \right)}}{{t + 1}}dt - 2\int {\frac{1}{{t + 1}}dt} }{/tex}

{tex}= 2\int {\left( {{t^2} - t + 1} \right)dt - 2\int {\frac{1}{{t + 1}}d} t} {/tex}

{tex}= 2\left[ {\frac{{{t^3}}}{3} - \frac{{{t^2}}}{2} + t - \log \left| {\left( {t + 1} \right)} \right|} \right] + C{/tex}

{tex}= 2\left[ {\frac{{x\sqrt x }}{2} - \frac{x}{2} + \sqrt x - \log \left| {\sqrt x + 1} \right|} \right] + C{/tex}

solution_text: Given integral is: {tex}\int \frac{\sqrt{a+x}}{\sqrt{a-x}} d x{/tex} Let {tex}I ={/tex} {tex}\int \frac{\sqrt{a+x}}{\sqrt{a-x}} d x{/tex} = {tex}\int \frac{\sqrt{a+x}}{\sqrt{a-x}} \times \frac{\sqrt{a+x}}{\sqrt{a+x}} d x{/tex} = {tex}\int \frac{a+x}{\sqrt{a^{2}-x^{2}}} d x{/tex} = {tex}\text { a } \int \frac{1}{\sqrt{a^{2}-x^{2}}} d x-\frac{1}{2} \int \frac{-2 x}{\sqrt{a^{2}-x^{2}}} d x{/tex} Let t = a 2 - x 2 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} -2x dx = dt {tex}\therefore~~ I{/tex} {tex}={/tex} {tex}a \sin^{-1} \left(\frac{x}{a}\right)-\frac{1}{2} \int \frac{1}{\sqrt{t}} d t{/tex} = {tex}\operatorname{a.sin}^{-1}\left(\frac{x}{a}\right)-\frac{1}{2} \times 2 \sqrt{t}{/tex} = {tex}\operatorname{a.sin}^{-1}\left(\frac{x}{a}\right)-\sqrt{a^{2}-x^{2}}+C{/tex}
solution_html:
Given integral is: {tex}\int \frac{\sqrt{a+x}}{\sqrt{a-x}} d x{/tex}
Let {tex}I ={/tex}{tex}\int \frac{\sqrt{a+x}}{\sqrt{a-x}} d x{/tex} 
= {tex}\int \frac{\sqrt{a+x}}{\sqrt{a-x}} \times \frac{\sqrt{a+x}}{\sqrt{a+x}} d x{/tex} 
= {tex}\int \frac{a+x}{\sqrt{a^{2}-x^{2}}} d x{/tex} 
= {tex}\text { a } \int \frac{1}{\sqrt{a^{2}-x^{2}}} d x-\frac{1}{2} \int \frac{-2 x}{\sqrt{a^{2}-x^{2}}} d x{/tex} 
Let t = a2 - x2 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} -2x dx = dt 
{tex}\therefore~~ I{/tex} {tex}={/tex} {tex}a \sin^{-1} \left(\frac{x}{a}\right)-\frac{1}{2} \int \frac{1}{\sqrt{t}} d t{/tex} 
= {tex}\operatorname{a.sin}^{-1}\left(\frac{x}{a}\right)-\frac{1}{2} \times 2 \sqrt{t}{/tex} 
= {tex}\operatorname{a.sin}^{-1}\left(\frac{x}{a}\right)-\sqrt{a^{2}-x^{2}}+C{/tex}

solution_text: Let {tex}I = \int {\frac{{{x^{\frac{1}{2}}}}}{{1 + {x^{\frac{3}{4}}}}}dx} {/tex} Put {tex}x = {t^4} \Rightarrow dx = 4{t^3}dt{/tex} {tex}\therefore I = 4\int {\frac{{{t^2}\left( {{t^3}} \right)}}{{1 + {t^3}}}dt = 4\int {\left( {{t^2} - \frac{{{t^2}}}{{1 + {t^3}}}} \right)dt} } {/tex} {tex}I = 4\int {{t^2}dt - 4\int {\frac{{{t^2}}}{{1 + {t^3}}}dt} } {/tex} I = I 1 - I 2 {tex}{I_1} = 4\int {{t^2}dt = 4.\frac{{{t^3}}}{3} + {C_1} = \frac{4}{3}{x^{3/4}} + {C_1}{\kern 1pt} }{/tex} Now, {tex}{I_2} = 4\int {\frac{{{t^2}}}{{1 + {t^3}}}dt} {/tex} Again, put {tex}1 + {t^3} = z \Rightarrow 3{t^2}dt = dz{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow {t^2}dt = \frac{1}{3}dz \\ I_2= \frac{4}{3}\int {\frac{1}{z}dz} {/tex} {tex}= \frac{4}{3}\log \left| z \right| + {C_2} = \frac{4}{3}log|1+t^3|+C_2{/tex} {tex}= \frac{4}{3}\log \left| {\left( {1 + {x^{3/4}}} \right)} \right| + {C_2}{/tex} {tex}\therefore I = \frac{4}{3}{x^{3/4}} + {C_1} - \frac{4}{3}\log \left| {\left( {1 + {x^{3/4}}} \right)} \right| - {C_2}{/tex} {tex} = \frac{4}{3}{x^{3/4}} - \frac{4}{3}\log \left| {\left( {1 + {x^{3/4}}} \right)} \right| +C{/tex} [ {tex}\because C_1-C_2=C{/tex} ]
solution_html:
Let {tex}I = \int {\frac{{{x^{\frac{1}{2}}}}}{{1 + {x^{\frac{3}{4}}}}}dx} {/tex}

Put {tex}x = {t^4} \Rightarrow dx = 4{t^3}dt{/tex}

{tex}\therefore I = 4\int {\frac{{{t^2}\left( {{t^3}} \right)}}{{1 + {t^3}}}dt = 4\int {\left( {{t^2} - \frac{{{t^2}}}{{1 + {t^3}}}} \right)dt} } {/tex}

{tex}I = 4\int {{t^2}dt - 4\int {\frac{{{t^2}}}{{1 + {t^3}}}dt} } {/tex}

I = I1 - I2

{tex}{I_1} = 4\int {{t^2}dt = 4.\frac{{{t^3}}}{3} + {C_1} = \frac{4}{3}{x^{3/4}} + {C_1}{\kern 1pt} }{/tex}

Now, {tex}{I_2} = 4\int {\frac{{{t^2}}}{{1 + {t^3}}}dt} {/tex}

Again, put {tex}1 + {t^3} = z \Rightarrow 3{t^2}dt = dz{/tex}

{tex}\Rightarrow {t^2}dt = \frac{1}{3}dz \\ I_2= \frac{4}{3}\int {\frac{1}{z}dz} {/tex}

{tex}= \frac{4}{3}\log \left| z \right| + {C_2} = \frac{4}{3}log|1+t^3|+C_2{/tex}

{tex}= \frac{4}{3}\log \left| {\left( {1 + {x^{3/4}}} \right)} \right| + {C_2}{/tex}

{tex}\therefore I = \frac{4}{3}{x^{3/4}} + {C_1} - \frac{4}{3}\log \left| {\left( {1 + {x^{3/4}}} \right)} \right| - {C_2}{/tex}

{tex} = \frac{4}{3}{x^{3/4}} - \frac{4}{3}\log \left| {\left( {1 + {x^{3/4}}} \right)} \right| +C{/tex} [{tex}\because C_1-C_2=C{/tex}]

solution_text: Let {tex}I = \int {\frac{{\sqrt {1 + {x^2}} }}{{{x^4}}}} dx = \int {\frac{{\sqrt {1 + {x^2}} }}{x}.\frac{1}{{{x^3}}}dx}{/tex} {tex}= \int {\sqrt {\frac{{1 + {x^2}}}{{{x^2}}}} .\frac{1}{{{x^3}}}} dx = \int {\sqrt {\frac{1}{{{x^2}}} + 1} .\frac{1}{{{x^3}}}dx} {/tex} Put {tex}1 + \frac{1}{{{x^2}}} = {t^2} \Rightarrow \frac{{ - 2}}{{{x^3}}}dx = 2tdt{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{1}{{{x^3}}} = -tdt{/tex} {tex}\therefore I = - \int {{t^2}dt = - \frac{{{t^3}}}{3} + C} {/tex} {tex}= - \frac{1}{3}{\left( {1 + \frac{1}{{{x^2}}}} \right)^{3/2}} + C{/tex}
solution_html:

Put {tex}1 + \frac{1}{{{x^2}}} = {t^2} \Rightarrow \frac{{ - 2}}{{{x^3}}}dx = 2tdt{/tex}

solution_text: Let {tex}I = \int {\frac{{dt}}{{\sqrt {3t - 2{t^2}} }}} = \frac{1}{{\sqrt 2 }}\frac{{dt}}{{\sqrt { - \left( {{t^2} - \frac{3}{2}t} \right)} }}{/tex} {tex}= \frac{1}{{\sqrt 2 }}\int {\frac{{dt}}{{\sqrt { - \left[ {\left( {{t^2} - 2.\frac{1}{2}.\frac{3}{2}t} \right) + {{\left( {\frac{3}{4}} \right)}^2} - {{\left( {\frac{3}{4}} \right)}^2}} \right]} }}} {/tex} {tex}= \frac{1}{{\sqrt 2 }}\int {\frac{{dt}}{{\sqrt { - \left[ {{{\left( {t - \frac{3}{4}} \right)}^2} - {{\left( {\frac{3}{4}} \right)}^2}} \right]} }}} {/tex} {tex}= \frac{1}{{\sqrt 2 }}\int {\frac{{dt}}{{\sqrt {{{\left( {\frac{3}{4}} \right)}^2} - {{\left( {t - \frac{3}{4}} \right)}^2}} }}} {/tex} {tex}= \frac{1}{{\sqrt 2 }}{\sin ^{ - 1}}\left( {\frac{{t - \frac{3}{4}}}{{\frac{3}{4}}}} \right) + C = \frac{1}{{\sqrt 2 }}{\sin ^{ - 1}}\left( {\frac{{4t - 3}}{3}} \right) + C{/tex}
solution_html:

{tex}= \frac{1}{{\sqrt 2 }}\int {\frac{{dt}}{{\sqrt { - \left[ {\left( {{t^2} - 2.\frac{1}{2}.\frac{3}{2}t} \right) + {{\left( {\frac{3}{4}} \right)}^2} - {{\left( {\frac{3}{4}} \right)}^2}} \right]} }}} {/tex}

solution_text: Given integral is: {tex}\int \frac{x}{x^{4}-1} d x{/tex} Let {tex}I=\int \frac{x}{x^{4}-1} d x{/tex} Also, Let; t = x 2 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} dt = 2x dx = {tex}\int \frac{1}{\left(t^{2}-1\right)} \frac{d t}{2}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{2} \int \frac{1}{(t+1)(t-1)} d t{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{2} \int \frac{1}{2}\left[\frac{1}{(t-1)}-\frac{1}{(t+1)}\right] d t{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{4}(\log |t-1|-\log |t+1|)+C{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{4} \log \left|\frac{t-1}{t+1}\right|+C{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{4} \log \left|\frac{x^{2}-1}{x^{2}+1}\right|+C{/tex}
solution_html:
Given integral is: {tex}\int \frac{x}{x^{4}-1} d x{/tex} 
Let {tex}I=\int \frac{x}{x^{4}-1} d x{/tex}
Also, Let; t = x2 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} dt = 2x dx
= {tex}\int \frac{1}{\left(t^{2}-1\right)} \frac{d t}{2}{/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{1}{2} \int \frac{1}{(t+1)(t-1)} d t{/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{1}{2} \int \frac{1}{2}\left[\frac{1}{(t-1)}-\frac{1}{(t+1)}\right] d t{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{1}{4}(\log |t-1|-\log |t+1|)+C{/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{1}{4} \log \left|\frac{t-1}{t+1}\right|+C{/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{1}{4} \log \left|\frac{x^{2}-1}{x^{2}+1}\right|+C{/tex}

solution_text: Let {tex}I =\int \frac{x^{2}}{1-x^{4}} d x{/tex} = {tex}\int \frac{1}{2}\left[\frac{2 x^{2}}{\left(1+x^{2}\right)\left(1-x^{2}\right)} d x\right]{/tex} = {tex}\int \frac{1}{2}\left[\frac{x^{2}+x^{2}-1+1}{\left(1+x^{2}\right)\left(1-x^{2}\right)} d x\right]{/tex} = {tex}\int \frac{1}{2}\left[\frac{\left(1+x^{2}\right)-\left(1-x^{2}\right)}{\left(1+x^{2}\right)\left(1-x^{2}\right)} d x\right]{/tex} = {tex}\int \frac{1}{2}\left[\frac{\left(1+x^{2}\right)}{\left(1+x^{2}\right)\left(1-x^{2}\right)} d x-\frac{\left(1-x^{2}\right)}{\left(1+x^{2}\right)\left(1-x^{2}\right)} d x\right]{/tex} = {tex}\int \frac{1}{2}\left[\frac{1}{\left(1-x^{2}\right)} d x-\frac{1}{\left(1+x^{2}\right)} d x\right]{/tex} As we know, {tex}\int \frac{1}{\left(a^{2}-x^{2}\right)} d x{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{2 a} \log \frac{a+x}{a-x}{/tex} {tex}\int \frac{1}{\left(a^{2}+x^{2}\right)} d x{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{a} \tan ^{-1} \frac{x}{a}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{2} \times \frac{1}{2} \log \frac{1+x}{1-x}-\frac{1}{2} \tan ^{-1} x+C{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{4} \log \frac{1+x}{1-x}-\frac{1}{2} \tan ^{-1} x+C{/tex}
solution_html:
Let {tex}I =\int \frac{x^{2}}{1-x^{4}} d x{/tex}
= {tex}\int \frac{1}{2}\left[\frac{2 x^{2}}{\left(1+x^{2}\right)\left(1-x^{2}\right)} d x\right]{/tex} 
= {tex}\int \frac{1}{2}\left[\frac{x^{2}+x^{2}-1+1}{\left(1+x^{2}\right)\left(1-x^{2}\right)} d x\right]{/tex} 
= {tex}\int \frac{1}{2}\left[\frac{\left(1+x^{2}\right)-\left(1-x^{2}\right)}{\left(1+x^{2}\right)\left(1-x^{2}\right)} d x\right]{/tex} 
= {tex}\int \frac{1}{2}\left[\frac{\left(1+x^{2}\right)}{\left(1+x^{2}\right)\left(1-x^{2}\right)} d x-\frac{\left(1-x^{2}\right)}{\left(1+x^{2}\right)\left(1-x^{2}\right)} d x\right]{/tex} 
= {tex}\int \frac{1}{2}\left[\frac{1}{\left(1-x^{2}\right)} d x-\frac{1}{\left(1+x^{2}\right)} d x\right]{/tex} 
As we know,
{tex}\int \frac{1}{\left(a^{2}-x^{2}\right)} d x{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{2 a} \log \frac{a+x}{a-x}{/tex} 
{tex}\int \frac{1}{\left(a^{2}+x^{2}\right)} d x{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{a} \tan ^{-1} \frac{x}{a}{/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{1}{2} \times \frac{1}{2} \log \frac{1+x}{1-x}-\frac{1}{2} \tan ^{-1} x+C{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{1}{4} \log \frac{1+x}{1-x}-\frac{1}{2} \tan ^{-1} x+C{/tex}

solution_text: Let {tex}I = \int {\frac{{{{\sin }^{ - 1}}x}}{{{{\left( {1 - {x^2}} \right)}^{\frac{3}{2}}}}}dx} = \int {\frac{{{{\sin }^{ - 1}}x}}{{\left( {1 - {x^2}} \right)\sqrt {1 - {x^2}} }}dx}{/tex} Put {tex}{\sin ^{ - 1}}x = t \Rightarrow \frac{1}{{\sqrt {1 - {x^2}} }}dx = dt{/tex} and {tex}x = \sin t \Rightarrow 1 - {x^2} = {\cos ^2}t{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow \cos t = \sqrt {1 - {x^2}} {/tex} {tex}\therefore I = \int {\frac{t}{{{{\cos }^2}t}}dt = \int {t.{{\sec }^2}tdt} } {/tex} {tex}= t.\int {{{\sec }^2}tdt - \int {\left( {\frac{d}{{dt}}t.\int {{{\sec }^2}tdt} } \right)} dt}{/tex} {tex}= t.\tan t - \int {1.\tan tdt}{/tex} {tex}= t.\tan t + \log \left| {\cos t} \right| + C{/tex} {tex}\left[ {\because \int {\tan xdx = - \log \left| {\cos x} \right| + C} } \right]{/tex} {tex}= {\sin ^{ - 1}}x.\frac{x}{{\sqrt {1 - {x^2}} }} + \log \left| {\sqrt {1 - {x^2}} } \right| + C{/tex}
solution_html:
Let {tex}I = \int {\frac{{{{\sin }^{ - 1}}x}}{{{{\left( {1 - {x^2}} \right)}^{\frac{3}{2}}}}}dx} = \int {\frac{{{{\sin }^{ - 1}}x}}{{\left( {1 - {x^2}} \right)\sqrt {1 - {x^2}} }}dx}{/tex}

Put {tex}{\sin ^{ - 1}}x = t \Rightarrow \frac{1}{{\sqrt {1 - {x^2}} }}dx = dt{/tex}

and {tex}x = \sin t \Rightarrow 1 - {x^2} = {\cos ^2}t{/tex}

{tex} \Rightarrow \cos t = \sqrt {1 - {x^2}} {/tex}

{tex}\therefore I = \int {\frac{t}{{{{\cos }^2}t}}dt = \int {t.{{\sec }^2}tdt} } {/tex}

{tex}= t.\int {{{\sec }^2}tdt - \int {\left( {\frac{d}{{dt}}t.\int {{{\sec }^2}tdt} } \right)} dt}{/tex}

{tex}= t.\tan t - \int {1.\tan tdt}{/tex}

{tex}= t.\tan t + \log \left| {\cos t} \right| + C{/tex} {tex}\left[ {\because \int {\tan xdx = - \log \left| {\cos x} \right| + C} } \right]{/tex}

{tex}= {\sin ^{ - 1}}x.\frac{x}{{\sqrt {1 - {x^2}} }} + \log \left| {\sqrt {1 - {x^2}} } \right| + C{/tex}

question_text: Evaluate {tex}\int \frac{(\cos 5 x+\cos 4 x)}{1-2 \cos 3 x} d x{/tex}
question_html: Evaluate {tex}\int \frac{(\cos 5 x+\cos 4 x)}{1-2 \cos 3 x} d x{/tex}
solution_text: Given integral is: {tex}\int \frac{(\cos 5 x+\cos 4 x)}{1-2 \cos 3 x} d x{/tex} [cos a + cos b = 2 cos {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex} (a + b) cos {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex} (a - b)] = {tex}\int \frac{2 \cos \frac{9 x}{2} \cos \frac{x}{2}}{1-2 \cos 3 x} d x{/tex} = {tex}\int \frac{2 \cos \frac{9 x}{2} \cos \frac{x}{2} \cos \frac{3 x}{2}}{(1-2 \cos 3 x) \cos \frac{3 x}{2}} d x{/tex} = {tex}\int \frac{2 \cos \frac{9 x}{2} \cos \frac{x}{2} \cos \frac{3 x}{2}}{\cos \frac{3 x}{2}-2 \cos 3 x \cos \frac{3 x}{2}} d x{/tex} [ {tex}\because{/tex} 2 cos a cos b = cos (a + b) + cos (a - b)] = {tex}\int \frac{2 \cos \frac{9 x}{2} \cos \frac{x}{2} \cos \frac{3 x}{2}}{\cos \frac{3 x}{2}-\cos \frac{9 x}{2}-\cos \frac{3 x}{2}} d x{/tex} = {tex}\int-\cos 2 x-\cos x d x{/tex} = {tex}-\frac{\sin 2 x}{2}-\sin x+C{/tex}
solution_html:
Given integral is: {tex}\int \frac{(\cos 5 x+\cos 4 x)}{1-2 \cos 3 x} d x{/tex} 
[cos a + cos b = 2 cos {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex}(a + b) cos {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex}(a - b)]
= {tex}\int \frac{2 \cos \frac{9 x}{2} \cos \frac{x}{2}}{1-2 \cos 3 x} d x{/tex} 
= {tex}\int \frac{2 \cos \frac{9 x}{2} \cos \frac{x}{2} \cos \frac{3 x}{2}}{(1-2 \cos 3 x) \cos \frac{3 x}{2}} d x{/tex} 
= {tex}\int \frac{2 \cos \frac{9 x}{2} \cos \frac{x}{2} \cos \frac{3 x}{2}}{\cos \frac{3 x}{2}-2 \cos 3 x \cos \frac{3 x}{2}} d x{/tex} 
[{tex}\because{/tex} 2 cos a cos b = cos (a + b) + cos (a - b)]
= {tex}\int \frac{2 \cos \frac{9 x}{2} \cos \frac{x}{2} \cos \frac{3 x}{2}}{\cos \frac{3 x}{2}-\cos \frac{9 x}{2}-\cos \frac{3 x}{2}} d x{/tex} 
= {tex}\int-\cos 2 x-\cos x d x{/tex} 
= {tex}-\frac{\sin 2 x}{2}-\sin x+C{/tex}

question_text: Evaluate {tex}\int \frac { \sin ^ { 6 } x + \cos ^ { 6 } x } { \sin ^ { 2 } x \cos ^ { 2 } x } d x.{/tex}
question_html: Evaluate {tex}\int \frac { \sin ^ { 6 } x + \cos ^ { 6 } x } { \sin ^ { 2 } x \cos ^ { 2 } x } d x.{/tex}
solution_text: Let {tex}I = \int \frac { \sin ^ { 6 } x + \cos ^ { 6 } x } { \sin ^ { 2 } x \cos ^ { 2 } x } d x{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow I = \int \frac { \left( \sin ^ { 2 } x \right) ^ { 3 } + \left( \cos ^ { 2 } x \right) ^ { 3 } } { \sin ^ { 2 } x \cos ^ { 2 } x } d x{/tex} {tex}= \int \frac { \left[ \begin{array} { l } { \left( \sin ^ { 2 } x + \cos ^ { 2 } x \right) ^ { 3 } } \\ { - 3 \sin ^ { 2 } x \cos ^ { 2 } x \left( \sin ^ { 2 } x + \cos ^ { 2 } x \right) } \end{array} \right] } { \sin ^ { 2 } x \cos ^ { 2 } x } d x{/tex} {tex}[\because a^3 + b^3 = (a + b)^3 - 3ab(a + b)]{/tex} {tex}= \int \frac { ( 1 ) ^ { 3 } - 3 \sin ^ { 2 } x \cos ^ { 2 } x } { \sin ^ { 2 } x \cos ^ { 2 } x } d x{/tex} [ {tex} \because{/tex} sin 2 x + cos 2 x = 1] {tex}= \int \frac { 1 } { \sin ^ { 2 } x \cos ^ { 2 } x } d x - 3 \int \frac { \sin ^ { 2 } x \cos ^ { 2 } x } { \sin ^ { 2 } x \cos ^ { 2 } x } d x{/tex} {tex}= \int \frac { \sin ^ { 2 } x + \cos ^ { 2 } x } { \sin ^ { 2 } x \cos ^ { 2 } x } d x - 3 \int 1 d x{/tex} {tex}= \int \left[ \frac { \sin ^ { 2 } x } { \sin ^ { 2 } x \cos ^ { 2 } x } + \frac { \cos ^ { 2 } x } { \sin ^ { 2 } x \cos ^ { 2 } x } \right] d x - 3 \int 1 d x{/tex} {tex}= \int \left( \sec ^ { 2 } x + \cot ^ { 2 } x \right) d x - 3 \int 1 d x{/tex} {tex}= \int \sec ^ { 2 } x d x + \int \cot ^ { 2 } x d x - 3 \int 1 d x{/tex} {tex}= tan\ x - cot x -3x +C{/tex}
solution_html:
Let {tex}I = \int \frac { \sin ^ { 6 } x + \cos ^ { 6 } x } { \sin ^ { 2 } x \cos ^ { 2 } x } d x{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow I = \int \frac { \left( \sin ^ { 2 } x \right) ^ { 3 } + \left( \cos ^ { 2 } x \right) ^ { 3 } } { \sin ^ { 2 } x \cos ^ { 2 } x } d x{/tex}
{tex}= \int \frac { \left[ \begin{array} { l } { \left( \sin ^ { 2 } x + \cos ^ { 2 } x \right) ^ { 3 } } \\ { - 3 \sin ^ { 2 } x \cos ^ { 2 } x \left( \sin ^ { 2 } x + \cos ^ { 2 } x \right) } \end{array} \right] } { \sin ^ { 2 } x \cos ^ { 2 } x } d x{/tex}
{tex}[\because a^3 + b^3 = (a + b)^3 - 3ab(a + b)]{/tex}
{tex}= \int \frac { ( 1 ) ^ { 3 } - 3 \sin ^ { 2 } x \cos ^ { 2 } x } { \sin ^ { 2 } x \cos ^ { 2 } x } d x{/tex}
[{tex} \because{/tex}sin2x + cos2 x = 1]
{tex}= \int \frac { 1 } { \sin ^ { 2 } x \cos ^ { 2 } x } d x - 3 \int \frac { \sin ^ { 2 } x \cos ^ { 2 } x } { \sin ^ { 2 } x \cos ^ { 2 } x } d x{/tex}
{tex}= \int \frac { \sin ^ { 2 } x + \cos ^ { 2 } x } { \sin ^ { 2 } x \cos ^ { 2 } x } d x - 3 \int 1 d x{/tex}
{tex}= \int \left[ \frac { \sin ^ { 2 } x } { \sin ^ { 2 } x \cos ^ { 2 } x } + \frac { \cos ^ { 2 } x } { \sin ^ { 2 } x \cos ^ { 2 } x } \right] d x - 3 \int 1 d x{/tex}
{tex}= \int \left( \sec ^ { 2 } x + \cot ^ { 2 } x \right) d x - 3 \int 1 d x{/tex}
{tex}= \int \sec ^ { 2 } x d x + \int \cot ^ { 2 } x d x - 3 \int 1 d x{/tex}
{tex}= tan\ x - cot x -3x +C{/tex}

solution_text: Let {tex}I = \int {\frac{{\cos x - \cos 2x}}{{1 - \cos x}}dx = \int {\frac{{2\sin \frac{{3x}}{2}.\sin \frac{x}{2}}}{{1 - 1 + 2{{\sin }^2}\frac{x}{2}}}dx} }{/tex} {tex}= 2\int {\frac{{\sin \frac{{3x}}{2}.\sin \frac{x}{2}}}{{2{{\sin }^2}\frac{x}{2}}}dx = \int {\frac{{\sin \frac{{3x}}{2}}}{{\sin \frac{x}{2}}}dx} } {/tex} {tex} = 2\int {\frac{{3\sin \frac{x}{2} - 4{{\sin }^3}\frac{x}{2}}}{{\sin \frac{x}{2}}}dx\,\left[ {\because \sin 3x = 3\sin x - 4{{\sin }^3}x} \right]} {/tex} {tex}= 3\int {dx - 4\int {{{\sin }^2}\frac{x}{2}dx = 3\int {dx - 4\int {\frac{{1 - \cos x}}{2}dx} } } } {/tex} {tex} = 3\int {dx - 2\int {dx + 2\int {\cos xdx} } } {/tex} {tex}= \int {dx + 2\int {\cos xdx} } {/tex} = x + 2 sin x + C = 2 sin x + x + C
solution_html:
Let {tex}I = \int {\frac{{\cos x - \cos 2x}}{{1 - \cos x}}dx = \int {\frac{{2\sin \frac{{3x}}{2}.\sin \frac{x}{2}}}{{1 - 1 + 2{{\sin }^2}\frac{x}{2}}}dx} }{/tex}
{tex}= 2\int {\frac{{\sin \frac{{3x}}{2}.\sin \frac{x}{2}}}{{2{{\sin }^2}\frac{x}{2}}}dx = \int {\frac{{\sin \frac{{3x}}{2}}}{{\sin \frac{x}{2}}}dx} } {/tex}
{tex} = 2\int {\frac{{3\sin \frac{x}{2} - 4{{\sin }^3}\frac{x}{2}}}{{\sin \frac{x}{2}}}dx\,\left[ {\because \sin 3x = 3\sin x - 4{{\sin }^3}x} \right]} {/tex}
{tex}= 3\int {dx - 4\int {{{\sin }^2}\frac{x}{2}dx = 3\int {dx - 4\int {\frac{{1 - \cos x}}{2}dx} } } } {/tex}
{tex} = 3\int {dx - 2\int {dx + 2\int {\cos xdx} } } {/tex}
{tex}= \int {dx + 2\int {\cos xdx} } {/tex} = x + 2 sin x + C = 2 sin x + x + C

solution_text: Let {tex}I = \int {\frac{{dx}}{{x\sqrt {{x^4} - 1} }}}{/tex} {tex}I = \int {\frac{{xdx}}{{{x^2}\sqrt {{{\left( {{x^2}} \right)}^2} - 1} }}} {/tex} Put {tex}{x^2} = \sec \theta \Rightarrow \theta = {\sec ^{ - 1}}{x^2}{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow 2xdx = \sec \theta .\tan \theta d\theta {/tex} {tex}\therefore I = \frac{1}{2}\int {\frac{{\sec \theta .\tan \theta }}{{\sec \theta \sqrt{\sec^2 \theta-1} }}d\theta = } \frac{1}{2}\int {d\theta = \frac{1}{2}\theta + C}{/tex} {tex}I = \frac{1}{2}\int {\frac{{\sec \theta .\tan \theta }}{{\sec \theta \tan \theta }}d\theta } {/tex} {tex}= \frac{1}{2}\int {d\theta = \frac{1}{2}\theta + C}{/tex} {tex}= \frac{1}{2}{\sec ^{ - 1}}{\left( x \right)^2} + C{/tex}
solution_html:
Let {tex}I = \int {\frac{{dx}}{{x\sqrt {{x^4} - 1} }}}{/tex}

{tex}I = \int {\frac{{xdx}}{{{x^2}\sqrt {{{\left( {{x^2}} \right)}^2} - 1} }}} {/tex}

Put {tex}{x^2} = \sec \theta \Rightarrow \theta = {\sec ^{ - 1}}{x^2}{/tex}

{tex} \Rightarrow 2xdx = \sec \theta .\tan \theta d\theta {/tex}

{tex}\therefore I = \frac{1}{2}\int {\frac{{\sec \theta .\tan \theta }}{{\sec \theta \sqrt{\sec^2 \theta-1} }}d\theta = } \frac{1}{2}\int {d\theta = \frac{1}{2}\theta + C}{/tex}

{tex}I = \frac{1}{2}\int {\frac{{\sec \theta .\tan \theta }}{{\sec \theta \tan \theta }}d\theta } {/tex} {tex}= \frac{1}{2}\int {d\theta = \frac{1}{2}\theta + C}{/tex}

{tex}= \frac{1}{2}{\sec ^{ - 1}}{\left( x \right)^2} + C{/tex}

question_text: Evaluate {tex}\int_{0}^{2}\left(x^{2}+3\right) d x{/tex} as limit of sum.
solution_text: Given integral is; {tex}\int_{0}^{2}\left(x^{2}+3\right) d x{/tex} We know {tex}\int_{a}^{b} f(x) d x=\lim \limits_{h \rightarrow \infty} h \sum\limits_{r=0}^{n-1} f(a+r h){/tex} Here a = 0 , b = 2 h = {tex}\frac{b-a}{n}=\frac{2-0}{n}=\frac{2}{n}{/tex} ⇒ nh = 2 = {tex}\mathop {\lim }\limits_{h \to 0} h\sum\limits_{r = 0}^{n - 1} f (rh){/tex} = {tex}\mathop {\lim }\limits_{h \to 0} h\sum\limits_{r = 0}^{n - 1} {\left( {3 + {r^2}{h^2}} \right)}{/tex} = {tex}\mathop {\lim }\limits_{h \to 0} h [ 3n + h^2\sum\limits_{r = 0}^{n - 1} {\left( {r^2} \right)}]{/tex} = {tex}\mathop {\lim }\limits_{h \to 0} h{/tex} (3n + h 2 {tex}\left(\frac{(\mathrm{n}-1)(\mathrm{n}-1+1)(2 \mathrm{n}-2+1)}{6}\right){/tex} = {tex}\mathop {\lim }\limits_{h \to 0} h\left( {3n + {h^2}\left( {\frac{{\left( {{n^2} - n} \right)(2n - 1)}}{6}} \right)} \right.{/tex} = {tex}\mathop {\lim }\limits_{h \to 0} h\left( {3n + {h^2}\left( {\frac{{2{n^3} - 3{n^2} + n}}{6}} \right)} \right.{/tex} = {tex}\mathop {\lim }\limits_{h \to 0} \left( {3nh + \left( {\frac{{2{n^3}{h^3} - 3{n^2}{h^2} + n{h^3}}}{6}} \right)} \right.{/tex} = {tex}\mathop {\lim }\limits_{h \to 0} \left( {3 \times 2 + \left( {\frac{{{{2 \times 2}^3} - {{3 \times 2}^2}h + 2{h^2}}}{6}} \right)} \right.{/tex} = {tex}6+\frac{16}{6}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{26}{3}{/tex}
solution_html:
Given integral is; {tex}\int_{0}^{2}\left(x^{2}+3\right) d x{/tex}
We know {tex}\int_{a}^{b} f(x) d x=\lim \limits_{h \rightarrow \infty} h \sum\limits_{r=0}^{n-1} f(a+r h){/tex} 
Here a = 0 , b = 2
h = {tex}\frac{b-a}{n}=\frac{2-0}{n}=\frac{2}{n}{/tex} 
⇒ nh = 2
= {tex}\mathop {\lim }\limits_{h \to 0} h\sum\limits_{r = 0}^{n - 1} f (rh){/tex} 
= {tex}\mathop {\lim }\limits_{h \to 0} h\sum\limits_{r = 0}^{n - 1} {\left( {3 + {r^2}{h^2}} \right)}{/tex} 
= {tex}\mathop {\lim }\limits_{h \to 0} h [ 3n + h^2\sum\limits_{r = 0}^{n - 1} {\left( {r^2} \right)}]{/tex}
= {tex}\mathop {\lim }\limits_{h \to 0} h{/tex}(3n + h2 {tex}\left(\frac{(\mathrm{n}-1)(\mathrm{n}-1+1)(2 \mathrm{n}-2+1)}{6}\right){/tex} 
= {tex}\mathop {\lim }\limits_{h \to 0} h\left( {3n + {h^2}\left( {\frac{{\left( {{n^2} - n} \right)(2n - 1)}}{6}} \right)} \right.{/tex} 
= {tex}\mathop {\lim }\limits_{h \to 0} h\left( {3n + {h^2}\left( {\frac{{2{n^3} - 3{n^2} + n}}{6}} \right)} \right.{/tex} 
= {tex}\mathop {\lim }\limits_{h \to 0} \left( {3nh + \left( {\frac{{2{n^3}{h^3} - 3{n^2}{h^2} + n{h^3}}}{6}} \right)} \right.{/tex} 
= {tex}\mathop {\lim }\limits_{h \to 0} \left( {3 \times 2 + \left( {\frac{{{{2 \times 2}^3} - {{3 \times 2}^2}h + 2{h^2}}}{6}} \right)} \right.{/tex}
= {tex}6+\frac{16}{6}{/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{26}{3}{/tex}

question_text: Evaluate {tex}\int_{0}^{2} e^{x} d x{/tex} as the limit of a sum.
question_html: Evaluate {tex}\int_{0}^{2} e^{x} d x{/tex} as the limit of a sum.
solution_text: We know that, {tex}\begin{equation} \int_{a}^{b} x d x \end{equation} =(b-a) \mathop {\lim }\limits_{n \to \infty } \frac{1}{n}(f(a)+f(a+h)+f(a+2 h)+ \ldots+f(a+(n-1) h)){/tex} {tex}Hence, \int_{0}^{2} e^{x} d x{/tex} = {tex}(2 - 0)\mathop {\lim }\limits_{n \to \infty } \frac{1}{n}\left[ {{e^0} + {e^{\frac{2}{n}}} + {e^{\frac{4}{n}}} + \ldots + {e^{\frac{{2n - 2}}{n}}}} \right]{/tex} Using the sum of n terms of a G.P., where a = 1, r = {tex}e^{\frac{2}{n}}{/tex} , we have {tex}\int_{0}^{2} e^{x} d x{/tex} = {tex}2\mathop {\lim }\limits_{n \to \infty } \frac{1}{n}\left[ {\frac{{{e^{\frac{{2n}}{n}}} - 1}}{{{e^{\frac{2}{n}}} - 1}}} \right]{/tex} = {tex}2\mathop {\lim }\limits_{n \to \infty } \frac{1}{n}\left[ {\frac{{{e^2} - 1}}{{{e^{\frac{2}{n}}} - 1}}} \right]{/tex} = {tex}\frac{{2({e^2} - 1)}}{{\mathop {\lim }\limits_{n \to \infty } \left[ {\frac{{{e^{\frac{2}{n}}} - 1}}{{\frac{2}{n}}}} \right] \cdot 2}}{/tex} = e 2 - 1 [using {tex}\mathop {\lim }\limits_{h \to 0} \frac{{\left( {{e^h} - 1} \right)}}{h} = 1{/tex} ]
solution_html:
We know that,
{tex}\begin{equation} \int_{a}^{b} x d x \end{equation} =(b-a) \mathop {\lim }\limits_{n \to \infty } \frac{1}{n}(f(a)+f(a+h)+f(a+2 h)+ \ldots+f(a+(n-1) h)){/tex} 
{tex}Hence, \int_{0}^{2} e^{x} d x{/tex} = {tex}(2 - 0)\mathop {\lim }\limits_{n \to \infty } \frac{1}{n}\left[ {{e^0} + {e^{\frac{2}{n}}} + {e^{\frac{4}{n}}} + \ldots + {e^{\frac{{2n - 2}}{n}}}} \right]{/tex} 
Using the sum of n terms of a G.P., where a = 1, r = {tex}e^{\frac{2}{n}}{/tex}, we have
{tex}\int_{0}^{2} e^{x} d x{/tex} = {tex}2\mathop {\lim }\limits_{n \to \infty } \frac{1}{n}\left[ {\frac{{{e^{\frac{{2n}}{n}}} - 1}}{{{e^{\frac{2}{n}}} - 1}}} \right]{/tex} = {tex}2\mathop {\lim }\limits_{n \to \infty } \frac{1}{n}\left[ {\frac{{{e^2} - 1}}{{{e^{\frac{2}{n}}} - 1}}} \right]{/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{{2({e^2} - 1)}}{{\mathop {\lim }\limits_{n \to \infty } \left[ {\frac{{{e^{\frac{2}{n}}} - 1}}{{\frac{2}{n}}}} \right] \cdot 2}}{/tex} 
= e2 - 1  [using {tex}\mathop {\lim }\limits_{h \to 0} \frac{{\left( {{e^h} - 1} \right)}}{h} = 1{/tex} ]

solution_text: Let {tex}I = \int_0^1 {\frac{{dx}}{{{e^x} + {e^{ - x}}}} = \int_0^1 {\frac{{{e^x}}}{{1 + {e^{2x}}}}dx} }{/tex} Put e x = t {tex} \Rightarrow {/tex} e x dx = dt When x=0, t=1 When x=1, t=e {tex}\therefore I = \int_1^e {\frac{{dt}}{{1 + {t^2}}} = \left[ {{{\tan }^{ - 1}}t} \right]_1^e} {/tex} = tan -1 e - tan -1 1 {tex}= {\tan ^{ - 1}}e - \frac{\pi }{4}{/tex}
solution_html:
Let {tex}I = \int_0^1 {\frac{{dx}}{{{e^x} + {e^{ - x}}}} = \int_0^1 {\frac{{{e^x}}}{{1 + {e^{2x}}}}dx} }{/tex}

Put ex = t

{tex} \Rightarrow {/tex} exdx = dt

When x=0, t=1

When x=1, t=e

{tex}\therefore I = \int_1^e {\frac{{dt}}{{1 + {t^2}}} = \left[ {{{\tan }^{ - 1}}t} \right]_1^e} {/tex}

= tan-1 e - tan-1 1

{tex}= {\tan ^{ - 1}}e - \frac{\pi }{4}{/tex}

solution_text: Let {tex}I= \int \limits _{0}^{\pi / 2} \frac{\tan x d x}{1+m^{2} \tan ^{2} x} d x{/tex} {tex}=\int \limits_{0}^{\pi / 2} \frac{\frac{\sin x}{\cos x}}{1+m^{2} \cdot \frac{\sin ^{2} x}{\cos ^{2} x}} d x{/tex} {tex}=\int \limits_{0}^{\pi / 2} \frac{\sin x \cos x d x}{1-\sin ^{2} x+m^{2} \sin ^{2} x} d x{/tex} {tex}=\int \limits_{0}^{\pi / 2} \frac{\sin x \cos x}{1+\left(m^{2}-1\right) \sin ^{2} x} d x{/tex} Put sin 2 x = t {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2 sinx cosx dx = dt When x {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} 0, then t {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} 0 and {tex}x \rightarrow \frac {\pi} {2}{/tex} then t {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} 1 {tex}\therefore I=\frac{1}{2} \int_{0}^{1} \frac{d t}{1+\left(m^{2}-1\right) t}{/tex} {tex}=\frac{1}{2}\left[\frac{1}{m^{2}-1} \log \left(1+\left(m^{2}-1\right) t\right)\right]_{0}^{1}{/tex} {tex}=\frac{1}{2\left(m^{2}-1\right)}\left[\log \left(1+\left(m^{2}-1\right)\right)-\log 1\right]{/tex} {tex}=\frac{1}{2\left(m^{2}-1\right)}\log \left(1+\left(m^{2}-1\right)\right){/tex}
solution_html:
Let {tex}I= \int \limits _{0}^{\pi / 2} \frac{\tan x d x}{1+m^{2} \tan ^{2} x} d x{/tex}
{tex}=\int \limits_{0}^{\pi / 2} \frac{\frac{\sin x}{\cos x}}{1+m^{2} \cdot \frac{\sin ^{2} x}{\cos ^{2} x}} d x{/tex}
{tex}=\int \limits_{0}^{\pi / 2} \frac{\sin x \cos x d x}{1-\sin ^{2} x+m^{2} \sin ^{2} x} d x{/tex}
{tex}=\int \limits_{0}^{\pi / 2} \frac{\sin x \cos x}{1+\left(m^{2}-1\right) \sin ^{2} x} d x{/tex}
Put sin2x = t {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2 sinx cosx dx = dt
When x {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} 0, then t {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} 0 and {tex}x \rightarrow \frac {\pi} {2}{/tex}
then t {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} 1
{tex}\therefore I=\frac{1}{2} \int_{0}^{1} \frac{d t}{1+\left(m^{2}-1\right) t}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{1}{2}\left[\frac{1}{m^{2}-1} \log \left(1+\left(m^{2}-1\right) t\right)\right]_{0}^{1}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{1}{2\left(m^{2}-1\right)}\left[\log \left(1+\left(m^{2}-1\right)\right)-\log 1\right]{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{1}{2\left(m^{2}-1\right)}\log \left(1+\left(m^{2}-1\right)\right){/tex}

solution_text: Given integral is: {tex}\int_{1}^{2} \frac{d x}{\sqrt{(x-1)(2-x)}}{/tex} Let {tex}I = \int_{1}^{2} \frac{d x}{\sqrt{(x-1)(2-x)}}{/tex} = {tex}\int_{1}^{2} \frac{d x}{\sqrt{-\left(x^{2}-3 x+2\right)}}{/tex} Using perfect square method for the denominator {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x 2 - 3x + 2 = x 2 - 3x + ( {tex}\frac{3}{2}{/tex} ) 2 - ( {tex}\frac{3}{2}{/tex} ) 2 + 2 = {tex}\left(x-\frac{3}{2}\right)^{2}-\frac{1}{4}{/tex} = {tex}\int_{1}^{2} \frac{d x}{\sqrt{\left(\frac{1}{2}\right)^{2}-\left(x-\frac{3}{2}\right)^{2}}}{/tex} We know that {tex}\int \frac{d x}{\sqrt{a^{2}-x^{2}}}=\sin ^{-1}\left(\frac{x}{a}\right)+C{/tex} = {tex}\left[\sin ^{-1}\left(\frac{\left(x-\frac{3}{2}\right)}{\frac{1}{2}}\right)\right]_{1}^{2}{/tex} = {tex}\left[\sin ^{-1}(2 x-3)\right]_{1}^{2}{/tex} = sin -1 (1) - sin -1 (-1) = {tex}\frac{\pi}{2}+\frac{\pi}{2}{/tex} = {tex}\pi{/tex}
solution_html:
Given integral is: {tex}\int_{1}^{2} \frac{d x}{\sqrt{(x-1)(2-x)}}{/tex} 
Let {tex}I = \int_{1}^{2} \frac{d x}{\sqrt{(x-1)(2-x)}}{/tex} 
= {tex}\int_{1}^{2} \frac{d x}{\sqrt{-\left(x^{2}-3 x+2\right)}}{/tex} 
Using perfect square method for the denominator
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x2 - 3x + 2 = x2 - 3x + ({tex}\frac{3}{2}{/tex})2 - ({tex}\frac{3}{2}{/tex})2 + 2
= {tex}\left(x-\frac{3}{2}\right)^{2}-\frac{1}{4}{/tex} 
= {tex}\int_{1}^{2} \frac{d x}{\sqrt{\left(\frac{1}{2}\right)^{2}-\left(x-\frac{3}{2}\right)^{2}}}{/tex} 
We know that {tex}\int \frac{d x}{\sqrt{a^{2}-x^{2}}}=\sin ^{-1}\left(\frac{x}{a}\right)+C{/tex} 
= {tex}\left[\sin ^{-1}\left(\frac{\left(x-\frac{3}{2}\right)}{\frac{1}{2}}\right)\right]_{1}^{2}{/tex} = {tex}\left[\sin ^{-1}(2 x-3)\right]_{1}^{2}{/tex} 
= sin-1(1) - sin-1 (-1)
= {tex}\frac{\pi}{2}+\frac{\pi}{2}{/tex} 
= {tex}\pi{/tex}

solution_text: Given integral is: {tex}\int_{0}^{1} \frac{x d x}{\sqrt{1+x^{2}}}{/tex} Now put 1 + x 2 = t {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2x dx = dt At x = 0, t = 1 and at x = 1, t = 2 {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{1}{2} \int_{1}^{2} \frac{d t}{\sqrt{t}}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{2}[2 \sqrt{t}]_{1}^{2}{/tex} = {tex}\sqrt{2}{/tex} - 1 {tex}\Rightarrow \int_{0}^{1} \frac{\mathrm{xdx}}{\sqrt{1+\mathrm{x}^{2}}}=\sqrt{2}-1{/tex}
solution_html:
Given integral is: {tex}\int_{0}^{1} \frac{x d x}{\sqrt{1+x^{2}}}{/tex} 
Now put 1 + x2 = t
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2x dx = dt
At x = 0, t = 1 and at x = 1, t = 2
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{1}{2} \int_{1}^{2} \frac{d t}{\sqrt{t}}{/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{1}{2}[2 \sqrt{t}]_{1}^{2}{/tex} 
= {tex}\sqrt{2}{/tex} - 1
{tex}\Rightarrow \int_{0}^{1} \frac{\mathrm{xdx}}{\sqrt{1+\mathrm{x}^{2}}}=\sqrt{2}-1{/tex}

question_text: Evaluate {tex}\int_{0}^{\pi} x \sin x \cos ^{2} x d x{/tex}
question_html: Evaluate {tex}\int_{0}^{\pi} x \sin x \cos ^{2} x d x{/tex}
solution_text: Let I = {tex}\int_{0}^{\pi} x \sin x \cos ^{2} x d x{/tex} .......(i) Using Property {tex}\int_{a}^{b} f(x) d x=\int_{a}^{b} f(a+b-x) d x{/tex} , we get {tex}I = \int_{0}^{\pi} x \sin x \cos ^{2} x d x{/tex} = {tex}\int_{0}^{\pi}(\pi-x) \sin (\pi-x) \cos ^{2}(\pi-x) d x{/tex} ...( {tex}\because{/tex} sin( {tex}\pi{/tex} - x) = sin x and cos( {tex}\pi{/tex} - x) = -cos x) = {tex}\int_{0}^{\pi} \pi \sin x \cos ^{2} x d x-\int_{0}^{\pi} x \sin x \cos ^{2} x d x{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} I = {tex}\int_{0}^{\pi} \pi \sin x \cos ^{2} x d x-I{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow 2 \mathrm{I}=\int_{0}^{\pi} \pi \sin \mathrm{x} \cos ^{2} \mathrm{x} \mathrm{d} \mathrm{x}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \int_{0}^{\pi} \pi \sin x \cos ^{2} x d x=\pi \int_{0}^{\pi} \sin x \cos ^{2} x d x{/tex} Now let cos x = t ⇒ - sin x dx = dt And, at x = 0, t = 1 and at x = {tex}\pi{/tex} , t = -1 {tex}\Rightarrow 2 \mathrm{I}=-\pi \int_{1}^{-1} \mathrm{t}^{2} \mathrm{dt}=-\pi\left[\frac{\mathrm{t}^{3}}{3}\right]_{1}^{-1}=\frac{2 \pi}{3}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow 2 \mathrm{I}=\frac{2 \pi}{3}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} I = {tex}\frac{\pi}{3}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \int_{0}^{\pi} x \sin x \cos ^{2} x d x=\frac{\pi}{3}{/tex}
solution_html:
Let I = {tex}\int_{0}^{\pi} x \sin x \cos ^{2} x d x{/tex} .......(i)
Using Property
{tex}\int_{a}^{b} f(x) d x=\int_{a}^{b} f(a+b-x) d x{/tex}, we get 
{tex}I = \int_{0}^{\pi} x \sin x \cos ^{2} x d x{/tex}
= {tex}\int_{0}^{\pi}(\pi-x) \sin (\pi-x) \cos ^{2}(\pi-x) d x{/tex}   ...({tex}\because{/tex}  sin({tex}\pi{/tex} - x) = sin x and cos({tex}\pi{/tex} - x) = -cos x)
= {tex}\int_{0}^{\pi} \pi \sin x \cos ^{2} x d x-\int_{0}^{\pi} x \sin x \cos ^{2} x d x{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} I = {tex}\int_{0}^{\pi} \pi \sin x \cos ^{2} x d x-I{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow 2 \mathrm{I}=\int_{0}^{\pi} \pi \sin \mathrm{x} \cos ^{2} \mathrm{x} \mathrm{d} \mathrm{x}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \int_{0}^{\pi} \pi \sin x \cos ^{2} x d x=\pi \int_{0}^{\pi} \sin x \cos ^{2} x d x{/tex} 
Now let cos x = t
⇒ - sin x dx = dt
And, at x = 0, t = 1
and at x = {tex}\pi{/tex}, t = -1
{tex}\Rightarrow 2 \mathrm{I}=-\pi \int_{1}^{-1} \mathrm{t}^{2} \mathrm{dt}=-\pi\left[\frac{\mathrm{t}^{3}}{3}\right]_{1}^{-1}=\frac{2 \pi}{3}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow 2 \mathrm{I}=\frac{2 \pi}{3}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} I = {tex}\frac{\pi}{3}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \int_{0}^{\pi} x \sin x \cos ^{2} x d x=\frac{\pi}{3}{/tex}

solution_text: Given {tex}\int_{0}^{\frac{1}{2}} \frac{d x}{\left(1+x^{2}\right) \sqrt{1-x^{2}}}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} Let x = sin {tex}\theta{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} dx = cos {tex}\theta{/tex} d {tex}\theta{/tex} At x = 0, {tex}\theta{/tex} = 0 and x = {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex} , {tex}\theta=\frac{\pi}{6}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \int_{0}^{\frac{1}{2}} \frac{d x}{\left(1+x^{2}\right) \sqrt{1-x^{2}}}{/tex} = {tex}\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{6}} \frac{\cos \theta d \theta}{\left(1+\sin ^{2} \theta\right) \sqrt{1-\sin ^{2} \theta}}{/tex} As 1 - sin 2 {tex}\theta{/tex} = cos 2 {tex}\theta{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{6}} \frac{\cos \theta d \theta}{\left(1+\sin ^{2} \theta\right) \sqrt{1-\sin ^{2} \theta}}{/tex} = {tex}\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{6}} \frac{\cos \theta d \theta}{\left(1+\sin ^{2} \theta\right) \sqrt{\cos ^{2} \theta}}{/tex} = {tex}\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{6}} \frac{\cos \theta d \theta}{\left(1+\sin ^{2} \theta\right) \cos \theta}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{6}} \frac{d \theta}{\left(1+\sin ^{2} \theta\right)}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{6}} \frac{\frac{1}{\cos ^{2} \theta}}{\frac{\left(1+\sin ^{2} \theta\right)}{\cos ^{2} \theta}} d \theta{/tex} = {tex}\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{6}} \frac{\sec ^{2} \theta d \theta}{\left(\sec ^{2} \theta+\tan ^{2} \theta\right)}{/tex} ⇒As sec 2 {tex}\theta{/tex} - tan 2 {tex}\theta{/tex} = 1 {tex}\Rightarrow \int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{6}} \frac{\sec ^{2} \theta d \theta}{\left(1+2 \tan ^{2} \theta\right)}{/tex} Now put tan {tex}\theta{/tex} = t {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} sec 2 {tex}\theta{/tex} d {tex}\theta{/tex} = dt At {tex}\theta{/tex} = 0, t = 0 at {tex}\theta=\frac{\pi}{6}, \mathrm{t}=\frac{1}{\sqrt{3}}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \int_{0}^{\frac{1}{\sqrt{3}}} \frac{d t}{\left(1+2 t^{2}\right)}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{2} \int_{0}^{\frac{1}{\sqrt{3}}} \frac{d t}{\left(\left(\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}}\right)^{2}+t^{2}\right)}{/tex} As {tex}\int \frac{d x}{x^{2}+a^{2}}=\frac{1}{a} \tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{x}{a}\right)+C{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{1}{2} \int_{0}^{\frac{1}{\sqrt{3}}} \frac{d t}{\left(\left(\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}}\right)^{2}+t^{2}\right)}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{2}\left[\frac{1}{\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}}} \tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{t}{\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}}}\right)\right]_{0}^{\frac{1}{\sqrt{3}}}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{\sqrt{2}}{2} \tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{\sqrt{2}}{\sqrt{3}}\right){/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \int_{0}^{\frac{1}{2}} \frac{d x}{\left(1+x^{2}\right) \sqrt{1-x^{2}}}=\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}} \tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{\sqrt{2}}{\sqrt{3}}\right){/tex}

Given {tex}\int_{0}^{\frac{1}{2}} \frac{d x}{\left(1+x^{2}\right) \sqrt{1-x^{2}}}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} Let x = sin {tex}\theta{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} dx = cos {tex}\theta{/tex} d{tex}\theta{/tex}
At x = 0, {tex}\theta{/tex} = 0 and x = {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex}, {tex}\theta=\frac{\pi}{6}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \int_{0}^{\frac{1}{2}} \frac{d x}{\left(1+x^{2}\right) \sqrt{1-x^{2}}}{/tex} = {tex}\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{6}} \frac{\cos \theta d \theta}{\left(1+\sin ^{2} \theta\right) \sqrt{1-\sin ^{2} \theta}}{/tex} 
As 1 - sin2 {tex}\theta{/tex} = cos2 {tex}\theta{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{6}} \frac{\cos \theta d \theta}{\left(1+\sin ^{2} \theta\right) \sqrt{1-\sin ^{2} \theta}}{/tex} = {tex}\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{6}} \frac{\cos \theta d \theta}{\left(1+\sin ^{2} \theta\right) \sqrt{\cos ^{2} \theta}}{/tex} 
= {tex}\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{6}} \frac{\cos \theta d \theta}{\left(1+\sin ^{2} \theta\right) \cos \theta}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{6}} \frac{d \theta}{\left(1+\sin ^{2} \theta\right)}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{6}} \frac{\frac{1}{\cos ^{2} \theta}}{\frac{\left(1+\sin ^{2} \theta\right)}{\cos ^{2} \theta}} d \theta{/tex} = {tex}\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{6}} \frac{\sec ^{2} \theta d \theta}{\left(\sec ^{2} \theta+\tan ^{2} \theta\right)}{/tex} 
⇒As sec2 {tex}\theta{/tex} - tan2 {tex}\theta{/tex} = 1
{tex}\Rightarrow \int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{6}} \frac{\sec ^{2} \theta d \theta}{\left(1+2 \tan ^{2} \theta\right)}{/tex} 
Now put tan {tex}\theta{/tex} = t
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} sec2 {tex}\theta{/tex} d{tex}\theta{/tex} = dt
At {tex}\theta{/tex} = 0, t = 0
at {tex}\theta=\frac{\pi}{6}, \mathrm{t}=\frac{1}{\sqrt{3}}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \int_{0}^{\frac{1}{\sqrt{3}}} \frac{d t}{\left(1+2 t^{2}\right)}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{2} \int_{0}^{\frac{1}{\sqrt{3}}} \frac{d t}{\left(\left(\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}}\right)^{2}+t^{2}\right)}{/tex}
As {tex}\int \frac{d x}{x^{2}+a^{2}}=\frac{1}{a} \tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{x}{a}\right)+C{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{1}{2} \int_{0}^{\frac{1}{\sqrt{3}}} \frac{d t}{\left(\left(\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}}\right)^{2}+t^{2}\right)}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{2}\left[\frac{1}{\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}}} \tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{t}{\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}}}\right)\right]_{0}^{\frac{1}{\sqrt{3}}}{/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{\sqrt{2}}{2} \tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{\sqrt{2}}{\sqrt{3}}\right){/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \int_{0}^{\frac{1}{2}} \frac{d x}{\left(1+x^{2}\right) \sqrt{1-x^{2}}}=\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}} \tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{\sqrt{2}}{\sqrt{3}}\right){/tex}

solution_text: Let {tex}I = \int {\frac{{{x^2}}}{{{x^4} - {x^2} - 12}}} dx{/tex} {tex}= \int {\frac{{{x^2}}}{{{x^4} - 4{x^2} + 3{x^2} - 12}}} dx{/tex} {tex} = \int {\frac{{{x^2}dx}}{{{x^2}\left( {{x^2} - 4} \right) + 3\left( {{x^2} - 4} \right)}}} dx{/tex} {tex}= \int {\frac{{{x^2}dx}}{{\left( {{x^2} - 4} \right) \left( {{x^2} + 3} \right)}}} dx{/tex} Now, {tex} \frac{{{x^2}}}{{\left( {{x^2} - 4} \right)\left( {{x^2} + 3} \right)}}\,\,={/tex} {tex} \frac{t}{{\left( {t - 4} \right)\left( {t + 3} \right)}}{/tex} [ {tex}putting\ x^2=t{/tex} ] Using partial fractions, {tex}\frac{t}{{\left( {t - 4} \right)\left( {t + 3} \right)}}\, = \frac{A}{{t - 4}} + \frac{B}{{t + 3}}{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow t = A\left( {t + 3} \right) + B\left( {t - 4} \right){/tex} On comparing the coefficient of t on both sides, we get A + B = 1 On comparing the constant term on both sides, we get {tex} 3A - 4B = 0{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow 3\left( {1 - B} \right) - 4B = 0{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow 3 - 3B - 4B = 0{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow 7B = 3{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow B = \frac{3}{7}{/tex} If {tex}B = \frac{3}{7}{/tex} then {tex}A + \frac{3}{7} = 1{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow A = 1 - \frac{3}{7} = \frac{4}{7}{/tex} {tex}\frac{{{x^2}}}{{\left( {{x^2} - 4} \right)\left( {{x^2} + 3} \right)}} = \frac{4}{{7\left( {{x^2} - 4} \right)}} + \frac{3}{{7\left( {{x^2} + 3} \right)}}{/tex} {tex}\therefore I = \frac{4}{7}\int {\frac{1}{{{x^2} - {{\left( 2 \right)}^2}}}dx + \frac{3}{7}\int {\frac{1}{{{x^2} + {{\left( {\sqrt 3 } \right)}^2}}}dx} } {/tex} {tex} = \frac{4}{7}.\frac{1}{{2.2}}\log \left| {\frac{{x - 2}}{{x + 2}}} \right| + \frac{3}{7}.\frac{1}{{\sqrt 3 }}{\tan ^{ - 1}}\frac{x}{{\sqrt 3 }} + C{/tex} {tex}= \frac{1}{7}\log \left| {\frac{{x - 2}}{{x + 2}}} \right| + \frac{{\sqrt 3 }}{7}{\tan ^{ - 1}}\frac{x}{{\sqrt 3 }} + C{/tex}
solution_html:
Let {tex}I = \int {\frac{{{x^2}}}{{{x^4} - {x^2} - 12}}} dx{/tex}
{tex}= \int {\frac{{{x^2}}}{{{x^4} - 4{x^2} + 3{x^2} - 12}}} dx{/tex}
{tex} = \int {\frac{{{x^2}dx}}{{{x^2}\left( {{x^2} - 4} \right) + 3\left( {{x^2} - 4} \right)}}} dx{/tex}
{tex}= \int {\frac{{{x^2}dx}}{{\left( {{x^2} - 4} \right) \left( {{x^2} + 3} \right)}}} dx{/tex}
Now, {tex} \frac{{{x^2}}}{{\left( {{x^2} - 4} \right)\left( {{x^2} + 3} \right)}}\,\,={/tex}{tex} \frac{t}{{\left( {t - 4} \right)\left( {t + 3} \right)}}{/tex} [{tex}putting\ x^2=t{/tex}]
Using partial fractions,
{tex}\frac{t}{{\left( {t - 4} \right)\left( {t + 3} \right)}}\, = \frac{A}{{t - 4}} + \frac{B}{{t + 3}}{/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow t = A\left( {t + 3} \right) + B\left( {t - 4} \right){/tex}
On comparing the coefficient of t on both sides, we get
A + B = 1
On comparing the constant term on both sides, we get
{tex} 3A - 4B = 0{/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow 3\left( {1 - B} \right) - 4B = 0{/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow 3 - 3B - 4B = 0{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow 7B = 3{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow B = \frac{3}{7}{/tex}
If {tex}B = \frac{3}{7}{/tex} then {tex}A + \frac{3}{7} = 1{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow A = 1 - \frac{3}{7} = \frac{4}{7}{/tex}
{tex}\frac{{{x^2}}}{{\left( {{x^2} - 4} \right)\left( {{x^2} + 3} \right)}} = \frac{4}{{7\left( {{x^2} - 4} \right)}} + \frac{3}{{7\left( {{x^2} + 3} \right)}}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore I = \frac{4}{7}\int {\frac{1}{{{x^2} - {{\left( 2 \right)}^2}}}dx + \frac{3}{7}\int {\frac{1}{{{x^2} + {{\left( {\sqrt 3 } \right)}^2}}}dx} } {/tex}
{tex} = \frac{4}{7}.\frac{1}{{2.2}}\log \left| {\frac{{x - 2}}{{x + 2}}} \right| + \frac{3}{7}.\frac{1}{{\sqrt 3 }}{\tan ^{ - 1}}\frac{x}{{\sqrt 3 }} + C{/tex}
{tex}= \frac{1}{7}\log \left| {\frac{{x - 2}}{{x + 2}}} \right| + \frac{{\sqrt 3 }}{7}{\tan ^{ - 1}}\frac{x}{{\sqrt 3 }} + C{/tex}

solution_text: Given: {tex}\int \frac{x^{2} d x}{\left(x^{2}+a^{2}\right)\left(x^{2}+b^{2}\right)}{/tex} Put x 2 = t, {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{x^{2}}{\left(x^{2}+a^{2}\right)\left(x^{2}+b^{2}\right)}=\frac{t}{\left(t+a^{2}\right)\left(t+b^{2}\right)}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{t}{\left(t+a^{2}\right)\left(t+b^{2}\right)}=\frac{A}{\left(t+a^{2}\right)}+\frac{B}{\left(t+b^{2}\right)}{/tex} (Concept of partial function) {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} t = t(A + B) + a 2 B + b 2 A On comparing coefficients of ‘t’ we get {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} A = {tex}\frac{a^{2}}{a^{2}-b^{2}}{/tex} and B = {tex}\frac{-b^{2}}{a^{2}-b^{2}}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{t}{\left(t+a^{2}\right)\left(t+b^{2}\right)}=\frac{1}{a^{2}-b^{2}}\left(\frac{a^{2}}{\left(t+a^{2}\right)}-\frac{b^{2}}{\left(t+b^{2}\right)}\right){/tex} Now put t = x 2 back in the above equation, {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{x^{2}}{\left(x^{2}+a^{2}\right)\left(x^{2}+b^{2}\right)}=\frac{1}{a^{2}-b^{2}}\left(\frac{a^{2}}{\left(x^{2}+a^{2}\right)}-\frac{b^{2}}{\left(x^{2}+b^{2}\right)}\right){/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \int \frac{x^{2} d x}{\left(x^{2}+a^{2}\right)\left(x^{2}+b^{2}\right)}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{a^{2}-b^{2}}\left(\int \frac{a^{2} d x}{\left(x^{2}+a^{2}\right)}-\int \frac{b^{2} d x}{\left(x^{2}+b^{2}\right)}\right){/tex} Now, {tex}\int \frac{d x}{x^{2}+a^{2}}=\frac{1}{a} \tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{x}{a}\right)+C{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{1}{a^{2}-b^{2}}\left(\int \frac{a^{2} d x}{\left(x^{2}+a^{2}\right)}-\int \frac{b^{2} d x}{\left(x^{2}+b^{2}\right)}\right){/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{a^{2}-b^{2}}\left(\frac{a^{2}}{a} \tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{x}{a}\right)+\frac{b^{2}}{b} \tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{x}{b}\right)+c\right){/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \int \frac{x^{2} d x}{\left(x^{2}+a^{2}\right)\left(x^{2}+b^{2}\right)}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{a^{2}-b^{2}}\left(a \tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{x}{a}\right)+b \tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{x}{b}\right)\right)+C{/tex}
solution_html:
Given: {tex}\int \frac{x^{2} d x}{\left(x^{2}+a^{2}\right)\left(x^{2}+b^{2}\right)}{/tex} 
Put x2 = t, 
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{x^{2}}{\left(x^{2}+a^{2}\right)\left(x^{2}+b^{2}\right)}=\frac{t}{\left(t+a^{2}\right)\left(t+b^{2}\right)}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{t}{\left(t+a^{2}\right)\left(t+b^{2}\right)}=\frac{A}{\left(t+a^{2}\right)}+\frac{B}{\left(t+b^{2}\right)}{/tex} (Concept of partial function)
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} t = t(A + B) + a2B + b2A
On comparing coefficients of ‘t’ we get
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} A = {tex}\frac{a^{2}}{a^{2}-b^{2}}{/tex} and B = {tex}\frac{-b^{2}}{a^{2}-b^{2}}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{t}{\left(t+a^{2}\right)\left(t+b^{2}\right)}=\frac{1}{a^{2}-b^{2}}\left(\frac{a^{2}}{\left(t+a^{2}\right)}-\frac{b^{2}}{\left(t+b^{2}\right)}\right){/tex} 
Now put t = x2 back in the above equation,
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{x^{2}}{\left(x^{2}+a^{2}\right)\left(x^{2}+b^{2}\right)}=\frac{1}{a^{2}-b^{2}}\left(\frac{a^{2}}{\left(x^{2}+a^{2}\right)}-\frac{b^{2}}{\left(x^{2}+b^{2}\right)}\right){/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \int \frac{x^{2} d x}{\left(x^{2}+a^{2}\right)\left(x^{2}+b^{2}\right)}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{a^{2}-b^{2}}\left(\int \frac{a^{2} d x}{\left(x^{2}+a^{2}\right)}-\int \frac{b^{2} d x}{\left(x^{2}+b^{2}\right)}\right){/tex} 
Now, {tex}\int \frac{d x}{x^{2}+a^{2}}=\frac{1}{a} \tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{x}{a}\right)+C{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{1}{a^{2}-b^{2}}\left(\int \frac{a^{2} d x}{\left(x^{2}+a^{2}\right)}-\int \frac{b^{2} d x}{\left(x^{2}+b^{2}\right)}\right){/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{1}{a^{2}-b^{2}}\left(\frac{a^{2}}{a} \tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{x}{a}\right)+\frac{b^{2}}{b} \tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{x}{b}\right)+c\right){/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \int \frac{x^{2} d x}{\left(x^{2}+a^{2}\right)\left(x^{2}+b^{2}\right)}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{a^{2}-b^{2}}\left(a \tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{x}{a}\right)+b \tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{x}{b}\right)\right)+C{/tex}

solution_text: Given {tex}I = \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi } \frac { x } { 1 + \sin x } d x{/tex} ....(i) {tex} \therefore \quad I = \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi } \frac { \pi - x } { 1 + \sin ( \pi - x ) } d x{/tex} {tex} \left[ \because \int _ { 0 } ^ { a } f ( x ) d x = \int _ { 0 } ^ { a } f ( a - x ) d x \right]{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow \quad I = \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi } \frac { \pi - x } { 1 + \sin x } d x{/tex} .....(ii) {tex} [ \because \sin ( \pi - x ) = \sin x ]{/tex} Adding Equations (i) and (ii) we get , {tex} 2 I = \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi } \frac { x + \pi - x } { 1 + \sin x } d x{/tex} {tex} = \pi \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi } \frac { 1 } { 1 + \sin x } d x{/tex} {tex} = \pi \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi } \frac { 1 } { 1 + \sin x } \times \frac { 1 - \sin x } { 1 - \sin x } d x{/tex} {tex} = \pi \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi } \frac { 1 - \sin x } { 1 - \sin ^ { 2 } x } d x{/tex} {tex} = \pi \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi } \frac { 1 - \sin x } { \cos ^ { 2 } x } d x{/tex} {tex}[cos^2x+sin^2x=1 \implies cos^2x=1-sin^2x ]{/tex} {tex} = \pi \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi } \left( \sec ^ { 2 } x - \tan x \sec x \right) d x{/tex} {tex} = \pi [ \tan x - \sec x ] _ { 0 } ^ { \pi }{/tex} {tex} = \pi [ ( \tan \pi - \sec \pi ) - ( \tan 0 - \sec 0 ) ]{/tex} {tex} = \pi{/tex} [ (0 - (-1) - (0 - 1)] {tex} = \pi ( 1 + 1 ){/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow 2 I = 2 \pi \quad \Rightarrow I = \pi{/tex}
solution_html:
Given {tex}I = \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi } \frac { x } { 1 + \sin x } d x{/tex} ....(i)
{tex} \therefore \quad I = \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi } \frac { \pi - x } { 1 + \sin ( \pi - x ) } d x{/tex}{tex} \left[ \because \int _ { 0 } ^ { a } f ( x ) d x = \int _ { 0 } ^ { a } f ( a - x ) d x \right]{/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow \quad I = \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi } \frac { \pi - x } { 1 + \sin x } d x{/tex} .....(ii){tex} [ \because \sin ( \pi - x ) = \sin x ]{/tex}
Adding Equations (i) and (ii) we get ,
{tex} 2 I = \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi } \frac { x + \pi - x } { 1 + \sin x } d x{/tex}
{tex} = \pi \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi } \frac { 1 } { 1 + \sin x } d x{/tex}
{tex} = \pi \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi } \frac { 1 } { 1 + \sin x } \times \frac { 1 - \sin x } { 1 - \sin x } d x{/tex}
{tex} = \pi \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi } \frac { 1 - \sin x } { 1 - \sin ^ { 2 } x } d x{/tex}
{tex} = \pi \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi } \frac { 1 - \sin x } { \cos ^ { 2 } x } d x{/tex} {tex}[cos^2x+sin^2x=1 \implies cos^2x=1-sin^2x ]{/tex}
{tex} = \pi \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi } \left( \sec ^ { 2 } x - \tan x \sec x \right) d x{/tex}
{tex} = \pi [ \tan x - \sec x ] _ { 0 } ^ { \pi }{/tex}
{tex} = \pi [ ( \tan \pi - \sec \pi ) - ( \tan 0 - \sec 0 ) ]{/tex}
{tex} = \pi{/tex} [ (0 - (-1) -  (0 - 1)]
{tex} = \pi ( 1 + 1 ){/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow 2 I = 2 \pi \quad \Rightarrow I = \pi{/tex}

solution_text: Given; {tex}\int \frac{2 x-1}{(x-1)(x+2)(x-3)} d x{/tex} Using the concept of partial fractions, {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{2 x-1}{(x-1)(x+2)(x-3)}=\frac{A}{(x-1)}+\frac{B}{(x+2)}+\frac{C}{(x-3)}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2x - 1 = x2(A + B + C) + x(C - 4B - A) + (3B - 2C - 6A) Comparing coefficient {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} A + B + C = 0 .......(i) {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} C - 4B - A = 2 ......(ii) {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 3B - 2C - 6A = -1 .....(iii) On solving (i), (ii) and (iii) we get A = {tex}-\frac{1}{6}{/tex} , B = {tex}-\frac{1}{3}{/tex} and C = {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{2 x-1}{(x-1)(x+2)(x-3)}=\frac{-\frac{1}{6}}{(x-1)}+\frac{-\frac{1}{3}}{(x+2)}+\frac{\frac{1}{2}}{(x-3)}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \int \frac{2 x-1}{(x-1)(x+2)(x-3)} d x{/tex} = {tex}\int \frac{1}{2(x-3)} d x-\int \frac{1}{3(x+2)} d x-\int \frac{1}{6(x-1)} d x{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{1}{2} \ln (x-3)-\frac{1}{3} \ln (x+2)-\frac{1}{6} \ln (x-1)+C{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \int \frac{2 x-1}{(x-1)(x+2)(x-3)} d x{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{2} \ln (x-3)-\frac{1}{3} \ln (x+2)-\frac{1}{6} \ln (x-1)+C{/tex}
solution_html:
Given; {tex}\int \frac{2 x-1}{(x-1)(x+2)(x-3)} d x{/tex} 
Using the concept of partial fractions,
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{2 x-1}{(x-1)(x+2)(x-3)}=\frac{A}{(x-1)}+\frac{B}{(x+2)}+\frac{C}{(x-3)}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2x - 1 = x2(A + B + C) + x(C - 4B - A) + (3B - 2C - 6A)
Comparing coefficient
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} A + B + C = 0 .......(i)
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} C - 4B - A = 2 ......(ii)
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 3B - 2C - 6A = -1 .....(iii)
On solving (i), (ii) and (iii) we get
A = {tex}-\frac{1}{6}{/tex}, B = {tex}-\frac{1}{3}{/tex} and C = {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{2 x-1}{(x-1)(x+2)(x-3)}=\frac{-\frac{1}{6}}{(x-1)}+\frac{-\frac{1}{3}}{(x+2)}+\frac{\frac{1}{2}}{(x-3)}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \int \frac{2 x-1}{(x-1)(x+2)(x-3)} d x{/tex} = {tex}\int \frac{1}{2(x-3)} d x-\int \frac{1}{3(x+2)} d x-\int \frac{1}{6(x-1)} d x{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{1}{2} \ln (x-3)-\frac{1}{3} \ln (x+2)-\frac{1}{6} \ln (x-1)+C{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \int \frac{2 x-1}{(x-1)(x+2)(x-3)} d x{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{2} \ln (x-3)-\frac{1}{3} \ln (x+2)-\frac{1}{6} \ln (x-1)+C{/tex}

solution_text: Let {tex}I = \int {{e^{{{\tan }^{ - 1}}x}}\left( {\frac{{1 + x + {x^2}}}{{1 + {x^2}}}} \right)} dx{/tex} {tex}= \int {{e^{{{\tan }^{ - 1}}x}}\left( {\frac{{1 + {x^2}}}{{1 + {x^2}}} + \frac{x}{{1 + {x^2}}}} \right)} dx{/tex} {tex}= \int {{e^{{{\tan }^{ - 1}}x}}\left( {1 + \frac{x}{{1 + {x^2}}}} \right)} dx{/tex} {tex}= \int {{e^{{{\tan }^{ - 1}}x}}+\int {\frac{{x{e^{{{\tan }^{ - 1}}x}}}}{{1 + {x^2}}}} } dx{/tex} Now, {tex}Let\ {I_1} = \int {\frac{{x{e^{{{\tan }^{ - 1}}x}}}}{{1 + {x^2}}}} dx{/tex} Put {tex}{\tan ^{ - 1}}x = t \Rightarrow x = \tan t{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow \frac{1}{{1 + {x^2}}}dx = dt{/tex} {tex}\therefore I_1 = \int {\mathop {\tan t}\limits.{e^t}\mathop {dt}\limits_{} }{/tex} {tex}= \tan t.{e^t} - \int {{{\sec }^2}t.{e^t}dt + C}{/tex} {tex}= \tan t.{e^t} - \int {\left( {1 + {{\tan }^2}t} \right){e^t}dt + C} \,\left[ {\because {{\sec }^2}\theta = 1 + {{\tan }^2}\theta } \right]{/tex} {tex}{I_1} = \tan t.{e^t} - \int {\left( {1 + {x^2}} \right)} \frac{{{e^{{{\tan }^{ - 1}}x}}}}{{1 + {x^2}}}dx + C{/tex} {tex}{I_1} = \tan t.{e^t} - \int {{e^{{{\tan }^{ - 1}}x}}dx + C} {/tex} {tex}\therefore I = \int {{e^{{{\tan }^{ - 1}}x}}dx + \tan t.{e^t} - \int {{e^{{{\tan }^{ - 1}}x}}dx + C} } {/tex} {tex}= \tan t.{e^t} + C{/tex} {tex}= x{e^{{{\tan }^{ - 1}}x}} + C{/tex}
solution_html:
Let {tex}I = \int {{e^{{{\tan }^{ - 1}}x}}\left( {\frac{{1 + x + {x^2}}}{{1 + {x^2}}}} \right)} dx{/tex}

{tex}= \int {{e^{{{\tan }^{ - 1}}x}}\left( {\frac{{1 + {x^2}}}{{1 + {x^2}}} + \frac{x}{{1 + {x^2}}}} \right)} dx{/tex}

{tex}= \int {{e^{{{\tan }^{ - 1}}x}}\left( {1 + \frac{x}{{1 + {x^2}}}} \right)} dx{/tex}

{tex}= \int {{e^{{{\tan }^{ - 1}}x}}+\int {\frac{{x{e^{{{\tan }^{ - 1}}x}}}}{{1 + {x^2}}}} } dx{/tex}

Now, {tex}Let\ {I_1} = \int {\frac{{x{e^{{{\tan }^{ - 1}}x}}}}{{1 + {x^2}}}} dx{/tex}

Put {tex}{\tan ^{ - 1}}x = t \Rightarrow x = \tan t{/tex}

{tex} \Rightarrow \frac{1}{{1 + {x^2}}}dx = dt{/tex}

{tex}\therefore I_1 = \int {\mathop {\tan t}\limits.{e^t}\mathop {dt}\limits_{} }{/tex}

{tex}= \tan t.{e^t} - \int {{{\sec }^2}t.{e^t}dt + C}{/tex}

question_html:
Evaluate {tex}\int {{{\sin }^{ - 1}}\sqrt {\frac{x}{{a + x}}} dx}{/tex}
(Hint: Put x = a tan2θ)
solution_text: Let {tex}I = \int {{{\sin }^{ -1}}\sqrt {\frac{x}{{a + x}}} } dx{/tex} Put {tex}x = a{\tan ^2}\theta{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow dx = 2a\tan \theta {\sec ^2}\theta d\theta {/tex} {tex}\therefore I = \int {{{\sin }^{ - 1}}\sqrt {\frac{{a{{\tan }^2}\theta }}{{a + a{{\tan }^2}\theta }}} } \left( {2a\tan \theta .{{\sec }^2}\theta } \right)d\theta {/tex} {tex}= 2a\int {{{\sin }^{ - 1}}\left( {\frac{{\tan \theta }}{{\sec \theta }}} \right)} \tan \theta .{\sec ^2}\theta d\theta {/tex} {tex}= 2a\int {{{\sin }^{ - 1}}\left( {\sin \theta } \right)} \tan \theta .{\sec ^2}\theta d\theta {/tex} {tex}= 2a\int {\mathop \theta \limits .\tan \theta \mathop {{{\sec }^2}}\limits_{} \theta d\theta }{/tex} {tex}= 2a\left[ {\theta .\int {\tan \theta .{{\sec }^2}\theta d\theta - \int {\left( {\frac{d}{{d\theta }}\theta .\int {\tan \theta .{{\sec }^2}\theta d\theta } } \right)d\theta } } } \right]{/tex} Let {tex}\tan \theta = t{/tex} {tex}{\sec ^2}\theta d\theta = dt{/tex} {tex}\int {\tan \theta {{\sec }^2}\theta d\theta = \int {tdt = \frac{{{t^2}}}{2} = \frac{{{{\tan }^2}\theta }}{2}} } {/tex} I {tex}= 2a\left[ {\theta .\frac{{{{\tan }^2}\theta }}{2} - \int {\frac{{{{\tan }^2}\theta }}{2}d\theta } } \right]{/tex} {tex}= a\theta {\tan ^2}\theta - a\int {\left( {{{\sec }^2}\theta - 1} \right)d\theta } {/tex} {tex} = a\theta {\tan ^2}\theta - a\tan \theta + a\theta + C{/tex} {tex}= a\left[ {\frac{x}{a}{{\tan }^{ - 1}}\sqrt {\frac{x}{a}} - \sqrt {\frac{x}{a}} + {{\tan }^{ - 1}}\sqrt {\frac{x}{a}} } \right] + C{/tex}
solution_html:
Let {tex}I = \int {{{\sin }^{ -1}}\sqrt {\frac{x}{{a + x}}} } dx{/tex}

Put {tex}x = a{\tan ^2}\theta{/tex}

{tex}\Rightarrow dx = 2a\tan \theta {\sec ^2}\theta d\theta {/tex}

{tex}\therefore I = \int {{{\sin }^{ - 1}}\sqrt {\frac{{a{{\tan }^2}\theta }}{{a + a{{\tan }^2}\theta }}} } \left( {2a\tan \theta .{{\sec }^2}\theta } \right)d\theta {/tex}

{tex}= 2a\int {{{\sin }^{ - 1}}\left( {\frac{{\tan \theta }}{{\sec \theta }}} \right)} \tan \theta .{\sec ^2}\theta d\theta {/tex}

{tex}= 2a\int {{{\sin }^{ - 1}}\left( {\sin \theta } \right)} \tan \theta .{\sec ^2}\theta d\theta {/tex}

{tex}= 2a\int {\mathop \theta \limits .\tan \theta \mathop {{{\sec }^2}}\limits_{} \theta d\theta }{/tex}

{tex}= 2a\left[ {\theta .\int {\tan \theta .{{\sec }^2}\theta d\theta - \int {\left( {\frac{d}{{d\theta }}\theta .\int {\tan \theta .{{\sec }^2}\theta d\theta } } \right)d\theta } } } \right]{/tex}

Let {tex}\tan \theta = t{/tex}

{tex}{\sec ^2}\theta d\theta = dt{/tex}

{tex}\int {\tan \theta {{\sec }^2}\theta d\theta = \int {tdt = \frac{{{t^2}}}{2} = \frac{{{{\tan }^2}\theta }}{2}} } {/tex}

I{tex}= 2a\left[ {\theta .\frac{{{{\tan }^2}\theta }}{2} - \int {\frac{{{{\tan }^2}\theta }}{2}d\theta } } \right]{/tex}

{tex}= a\theta {\tan ^2}\theta - a\int {\left( {{{\sec }^2}\theta - 1} \right)d\theta } {/tex}

{tex} = a\theta {\tan ^2}\theta - a\tan \theta + a\theta + C{/tex}

{tex}= a\left[ {\frac{x}{a}{{\tan }^{ - 1}}\sqrt {\frac{x}{a}} - \sqrt {\frac{x}{a}} + {{\tan }^{ - 1}}\sqrt {\frac{x}{a}} } \right] + C{/tex}

solution_text: let {tex}I = \int\limits_{\pi /3}^{\pi /2} {\frac{{\sqrt {1 + \cos x} }}{{{{\left( {1 - \ cosx} \right)}^{\frac{5}{2}}}}}} dx = \int_{\pi /3}^{\pi /2} {\frac{{{{\left( {2{{\cos }^2}\frac{x}{2}} \right)}^{1/2}}}}{{{{\left( {2{{\sin }^2}\frac{x}{2}} \right)}^{5/2}}}}} dx{/tex} {tex} = \frac{{\sqrt 2 }}{{4\sqrt 2 }}\int_{\pi /3}^{\pi /2} {\frac{{\cos \left( {\frac{x}{2}} \right)}}{{{{\sin }^5}\left( {\frac{x}{2}} \right)}}} dx = \frac{1}{4}\int_{\pi /3}^{\pi /2} {\frac{{\cos \left( {\frac{x}{2}} \right)}}{{{{\sin }^5}\left( {\frac{x}{2}} \right)}}} dx{/tex} Put {tex}\sin \frac{x}{2} = t{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow \cos \frac{x}{2}.\frac{1}{2}dx = dt{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow \cos \frac{x}{2}dx = 2dt{/tex} As {tex}x \to \frac{\pi }{3},{/tex} then {tex}t \to \frac{1}{2}{/tex} and {tex}x \to \frac{\pi }{2},{/tex} then {tex}t \to \frac{1}{{\sqrt 2 }}{/tex} {tex}\therefore I = \frac{2}{4}\int_{1/2}^{1/\sqrt 2 } {\frac{{dt}}{{{t^5}}} = \frac{1}{2}} \left[ {\frac{{{t^{ - 5 + 1}}}}{{ - 5 + 1}}} \right]_{1/2}^{1/\sqrt 2 }{/tex} {tex} = - \frac{1}{8}\left[ {\frac{1}{{{{\left( {\frac{1}{{\sqrt 2 }}} \right)}^4}}} - \frac{1}{{{{\left( {\frac{1}{2}} \right)}^4}}}} \right]{/tex} {tex} = - \frac{1}{8}\left( {4 - 16} \right) = \frac{{12}}{8} = \frac{3}{2}{/tex}

let {tex}I = \int\limits_{\pi /3}^{\pi /2} {\frac{{\sqrt {1 + \cos x} }}{{{{\left( {1 - \ cosx} \right)}^{\frac{5}{2}}}}}} dx = \int_{\pi /3}^{\pi /2} {\frac{{{{\left( {2{{\cos }^2}\frac{x}{2}} \right)}^{1/2}}}}{{{{\left( {2{{\sin }^2}\frac{x}{2}} \right)}^{5/2}}}}} dx{/tex}

{tex} = \frac{{\sqrt 2 }}{{4\sqrt 2 }}\int_{\pi /3}^{\pi /2} {\frac{{\cos \left( {\frac{x}{2}} \right)}}{{{{\sin }^5}\left( {\frac{x}{2}} \right)}}} dx = \frac{1}{4}\int_{\pi /3}^{\pi /2} {\frac{{\cos \left( {\frac{x}{2}} \right)}}{{{{\sin }^5}\left( {\frac{x}{2}} \right)}}} dx{/tex}

Put {tex}\sin \frac{x}{2} = t{/tex}

{tex} \Rightarrow \cos \frac{x}{2}.\frac{1}{2}dx = dt{/tex}

{tex} \Rightarrow \cos \frac{x}{2}dx = 2dt{/tex}

As {tex}x \to \frac{\pi }{3},{/tex} then {tex}t \to \frac{1}{2}{/tex}

and {tex}x \to \frac{\pi }{2},{/tex} then {tex}t \to \frac{1}{{\sqrt 2 }}{/tex}

{tex}\therefore I = \frac{2}{4}\int_{1/2}^{1/\sqrt 2 } {\frac{{dt}}{{{t^5}}} = \frac{1}{2}} \left[ {\frac{{{t^{ - 5 + 1}}}}{{ - 5 + 1}}} \right]_{1/2}^{1/\sqrt 2 }{/tex}

{tex} = - \frac{1}{8}\left[ {\frac{1}{{{{\left( {\frac{1}{{\sqrt 2 }}} \right)}^4}}} - \frac{1}{{{{\left( {\frac{1}{2}} \right)}^4}}}} \right]{/tex}

{tex} = - \frac{1}{8}\left( {4 - 16} \right) = \frac{{12}}{8} = \frac{3}{2}{/tex}

question_text: Evaluate {tex}\int e^{-3 x} \cos ^{3} x d x{/tex}
question_html: Evaluate {tex}\int e^{-3 x} \cos ^{3} x d x{/tex}
solution_text: Given integral is: {tex}\int e^{-3 x} \cos ^{3} x d x{/tex} Using trigonometric identity cos 3x = 4 cos 3 x - 3 cos x {tex}\Rightarrow \int e^{-3 x} \cos ^{3} x d x{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{4} \int e^{-3 x}(\cos 3 x+3 \cos x) d x{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{1}{4} \int e^{-3 x}(\cos 3 x+3 \cos x) d x{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{4} \int e^{-3 x} \cos 3 x d x+\frac{3}{4} \int e^{-3 x} \cos x d x{/tex} ...(i) Using a general formula i.e. {tex}\Rightarrow \int e^{a x} \cos b x d x{/tex} = {tex}\frac{e^{a x}}{a^{2}+b^{2}}{/tex} (a cos bx + b sin bx) {tex}\Rightarrow \int e^{-3 x} \cos 3 x d x{/tex} = {tex}\frac{e^{-3 x}}{(-3)^{2}+3^{2}}{/tex} ((-3) cos 3x + 3 sin 3x) {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{e^{-3 x}}{(-3)^{2}+3^{2}}{/tex} ((-3) cos 3x + 3 sin 3x) = {tex}\frac{e^{-3 x}}{6}{/tex} (sin 3x - cos 3x) .....(ii) {tex}\Rightarrow \int e^{-3 x} \cos x d x{/tex} = {tex}\frac{e^{-3 x}}{(-3)^{2}+1^{2}}{/tex} ((-3) cos 3x + 3 sin 3x) = {tex}\frac{e^{-3 x}}{10}{/tex} (sin x - 3 cos x) {tex}= \frac{e^{-3 x}}{10}{/tex} (sin x - 3 cos x) .....(iii) On putting (ii) and (iii) in (i) {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{1}{4} \int e^{-3 x} \cos 3 x d x{/tex} + {tex}\frac{3}{4} \int e^{-3 x} \cos x d x{/tex} = {tex}\frac{e^{-3 x}}{4 \times 6}(\sin 3 x-\cos 3 x){/tex} + {tex}\frac{3 e^{-3 x}}{4 \times 10}{/tex} (sin x - cos x) {tex}\Rightarrow \int e^{-3 x} \cos ^{3} x d x{/tex} = {tex}\mathrm{e}^{-3 \mathrm{x}}\left\{\frac{(\sin 3 \mathrm{x}-\cos 3 \mathrm{x})}{24}+\frac{3(\sin \mathrm{x}-3 \cos \mathrm{x})}{40}\right\}+\mathrm{C}{/tex}
solution_html:
Given integral is: {tex}\int e^{-3 x} \cos ^{3} x d x{/tex} 
Using trigonometric identity cos 3x = 4 cos3 x - 3 cos x
{tex}\Rightarrow \int e^{-3 x} \cos ^{3} x d x{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{4} \int e^{-3 x}(\cos 3 x+3 \cos x) d x{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{1}{4} \int e^{-3 x}(\cos 3 x+3 \cos x) d x{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{4} \int e^{-3 x} \cos 3 x d x+\frac{3}{4} \int e^{-3 x} \cos x d x{/tex}  ...(i)
Using a general formula i.e.
{tex}\Rightarrow \int e^{a x} \cos b x d x{/tex} = {tex}\frac{e^{a x}}{a^{2}+b^{2}}{/tex} (a cos bx + b sin bx)
{tex}\Rightarrow \int e^{-3 x} \cos 3 x d x{/tex} = {tex}\frac{e^{-3 x}}{(-3)^{2}+3^{2}}{/tex} ((-3) cos 3x + 3 sin 3x)
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{e^{-3 x}}{(-3)^{2}+3^{2}}{/tex} ((-3) cos 3x + 3 sin 3x) = {tex}\frac{e^{-3 x}}{6}{/tex} (sin 3x - cos 3x) .....(ii)
{tex}\Rightarrow \int e^{-3 x} \cos x d x{/tex} = {tex}\frac{e^{-3 x}}{(-3)^{2}+1^{2}}{/tex} ((-3) cos 3x + 3 sin 3x) = {tex}\frac{e^{-3 x}}{10}{/tex} (sin x - 3 cos x)
{tex}= \frac{e^{-3 x}}{10}{/tex} (sin x - 3 cos x) .....(iii)
On putting (ii) and (iii) in (i)
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{1}{4} \int e^{-3 x} \cos 3 x d x{/tex} + {tex}\frac{3}{4} \int e^{-3 x} \cos x d x{/tex} = {tex}\frac{e^{-3 x}}{4 \times 6}(\sin 3 x-\cos 3 x){/tex} + {tex}\frac{3 e^{-3 x}}{4 \times 10}{/tex} (sin x - cos x)
{tex}\Rightarrow \int e^{-3 x} \cos ^{3} x d x{/tex} = {tex}\mathrm{e}^{-3 \mathrm{x}}\left\{\frac{(\sin 3 \mathrm{x}-\cos 3 \mathrm{x})}{24}+\frac{3(\sin \mathrm{x}-3 \cos \mathrm{x})}{40}\right\}+\mathrm{C}{/tex}

Given {tex}\int \sqrt{\tan x} d x{/tex} 
Put tan x = t2 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} sec2 x dx = 2tdt

{tex}\Rightarrow \int \frac{2 t^{2} d t}{1+t^{4}}=\int \frac{\left(2 t^{2}+1-1\right) d t}{1+t^{4}}=\int \frac{\left(t^{2}+1\right)+\left(t^{2}-1\right)}{1+t^{4}} d t{/tex}

{tex}\Rightarrow \int \frac{t^{2}\left(1+\left(\frac{1}{t^{2}}\right)\right)}{1+t^{4}} d t+\int \frac{t^{2}\left(1-\left(\frac{1}{t^{2}}\right)\right)}{1+t^{4}} d t{/tex} = {tex}\int \frac{\left(1+\left(\frac{1}{t^{2}}\right)\right)}{\frac{1}{t^{2}}+t^{2}} d t+\int \frac{\left(1-\left(\frac{1}{t^{2}}\right)\right)}{\frac{1}{t^{2}}+t^{2}} d t{/tex} .......(i)

solution_html:
Given {tex}\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \frac{d x}{\left(a^{2} \cos ^{2} x+b^{2} \sin ^{2} x\right)^{2}}{/tex} 
Dividing Numerator and Denominator by cos4 x
{tex}\Rightarrow \int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \frac{\left(1 / \cos ^{4} x\right) d x}{\left(\left(a^{2} \cos ^{2} x+b^{2} \sin ^{2} x\right) / \cos ^{2} x\right)^{2}}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \frac{\sec ^{4} x d x}{\left(a^{2}+b^{2} \tan ^{2} x\right)^{2}}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \frac{\sec ^{2} x \sec ^{2} x d x}{\left(a^{2}+b^{2} \tan ^{2} x\right)^{2}}{/tex} = {tex}\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \frac{\left(1+\tan ^{2} x\right) \sec ^{2} x d x}{\left(a^{2}+b^{2} \tan ^{2} x\right)^{2}}{/tex} 
Put tan x = t
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} sec2 x dx = dt
At x = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} t = 0 and x = {tex}\frac{\pi}{2}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} t = {tex}\infty{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \frac{\left(1+\tan ^{2} x\right) \sec ^{2} x d x}{\left(a^{2}+b^{2} \tan ^{2} x\right)^{2}}{/tex} = {tex}\int_{0}^{\infty} \frac{\left(1+t^{2}\right) d t}{\left(a^{2}+b^{2} t^{2}\right)^{2}}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \int_{0}^{\infty} \frac{\left(1+t^{2}\right) d t}{\left(a^{2}+b^{2} t^{2}\right)^{2}}=\frac{1}{b^{2}} \int_{0}^{\infty} \frac{\left(b^{2}+b^{2} t^{2}\right) d t}{\left(a^{2}+b^{2} t^{2}\right)^{2}}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{1}{b^{2}} \int_{0}^{\infty} \frac{\left(b^{2}+b^{2} t^{2}\right) d t}{\left(a^{2}+b^{2} t^{2}\right)^{2}}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{b^{2}} \int_{0}^{\infty} \frac{\left(a^{2}+b^{2} t^{2}\right)+\left(b^{2}-a^{2}\right)}{\left(a^{2}+b^{2} t^{2}\right)^{2}} d t{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{1}{b^{2}} \int_{0}^{\infty} \frac{\left(a^{2}+b^{2} t^{2}\right)+\left(b^{2}-a^{2}\right)}{\left(a^{2}+b^{2} t^{2}\right)^{2}} d t{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{b^{2}} \int_{0}^{\infty} \frac{1}{\left(a^{2}+b^{2} t^{2}\right)} d t+\frac{1}{b^{2}} \int_{0}^{\infty} \frac{\left(b^{2}-a^{2}\right)}{\left(a^{2}+b^{2} t^{2}\right)^{2}} d t{/tex}
Let I = {tex}\frac{1}{b^{2}} \int_{0}^{\infty} \frac{1}{\left(a^{2}+b^{2} t^{2}\right)} d t{/tex} + {tex}\frac{1}{b^{2}} \int_{0}^{\infty} \frac{\left(b^{2}-a^{2}\right)}{\left(a^{2}+b^{2} t^{2}\right)^{2}} d t{/tex} ......(i)
Let I1 = {tex}\int_{0}^{\infty} \frac{1}{\left(a^{2}+b^{2} t^{2}\right)} d t{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \int_{0}^{\infty} \frac{1}{\left(a^{2}+b^{2} t^{2}\right)} d t{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{b^{2}} \int_{0}^{\infty} \frac{1}{\left(\left(a^{2} / b^{2}\right)+t^{2}\right)} d t{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} I1 = {tex}\frac{1}{b^{2}} \int_{0}^{\infty} \frac{1}{\left(\left(a^{2} / b^{2}\right)+t^{2}\right)} d t{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{b^{2}}\left(\frac{b}{a}\right)\left[\tan ^{-}\left(\frac{a t}{b}\right)\right]_{0}^{\infty}=\frac{\pi}{2 a b}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} I1 = {tex}\frac{\pi}{2 a b}{/tex} ......(ii)
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} I2 = {tex}\int_{0}^{\infty} \frac{1}{\left(a^{2}+b^{2} t^{2}\right)^{2}} d t{/tex} 
Let bt = a tan {tex}\theta{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} bdt = a sec2 {tex}\theta{/tex} d{tex}\theta{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} I2 = {tex}\frac{1}{b} \int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \frac{\operatorname{asec}^{2} \theta d \theta}{\left(a^{2}+a^{2} \tan ^{2} \theta\right)^{2}}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{b} \int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \frac{a \sec ^{2} \theta d \theta}{a^{4}\left(1+\tan ^{2} \theta\right)^{2}}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{a^{3} b} \int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \frac{\sec ^{2} \theta d \theta}{\sec ^{4} \theta}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{1}{a^{3} b} \int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \frac{\sec ^{2} \theta d \theta}{\sec ^{4} \theta}=\frac{1}{a^{3} b} \int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \cos ^{2} \theta d \theta{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{2 a^{2} b} \int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}}(1+\cos 2 \theta) d \theta{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{1}{2 a^{3} b} \int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}}(1+\cos 2 \theta) d \theta{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{2 a^{3} b}\left[\theta+\frac{\sin 2 \theta}{2}\right]_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}}=\frac{\pi}{4 a^{3} b}{/tex} .....(ii)
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} I = {tex}\frac{1}{b^{2}}\left(I_{1}+\left(b^{2}-a^{2}\right) I_{2}\right){/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{b^{2}}\left(\frac{\pi}{2 a b}+\left(b^{2}-a^{2}\right) \frac{\pi}{4 a^{3} b}\right){/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} I = {tex}\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \frac{d x}{\left(a^{2} \cos ^{2} x+b^{2} \sin ^{2} x\right)^{2}}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{\pi}{2 \mathrm{ab}^{2}}\left(1+\left(\frac{\mathrm{b}^{2}}{\mathrm{a}^{2}}-1\right) \pi\right){/tex}

solution_text: Given integral is: {tex}\int_{0}^{1} x \ln (1+2 x) d x{/tex} Let 1 + 2x = t {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2dx = dt At x = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} t = 1 and at x = 1 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} t = 3 {tex}\Rightarrow \int_{0}^{1} x \ln (1+2 x) d x{/tex} = {tex}\int_{1}^{3}(t-1) \ln t d t{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\int_{1}^{3}(t-1){/tex} In t dt = {tex}\int_{1}^{3} t \ln t d t-\int_{1}^{3} \ln t d t{/tex} ......(i) Apply Integration by parts {tex}\Rightarrow \int t\ln\ t d t{/tex} = {tex}\ln \mathrm{t} \int \mathrm{td} \mathrm{t}-\int \frac{\mathrm{d}}{\mathrm{dt}}(\ln \mathrm{t})\left(\int \mathrm{tdt}\right) \mathrm{d} \mathrm{t}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{t^{2}}{2} \ln t-\frac{t^{2}}{4}{/tex} .....(ii) {tex}\Rightarrow \int \ln t d t{/tex} = {tex}\ln \mathrm{t} \int \mathrm{dt}-\int \frac{\mathrm{d}}{\mathrm{dt}}(\ln \mathrm{t})\left(\int \mathrm{dt}\right) \mathrm{d} \mathrm{t}{/tex} = t In t - t ......(iii) Put (ii) and (iii) in (i) {tex}\Rightarrow \int_{1}^{3} t \ln t d t-\int_{1}^{3} \ln t d t{/tex} = {tex}\left[\left(\frac{t^{2}}{2} \ln t-\frac{t^{2}}{4}\right)-(t \ln t-t)\right]_{1}^{3}=\frac{3}{2} \ln 3{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \int_{0}^{1} x \ln (1+2 x) d x=\frac{3}{2} \ln 3{/tex}
solution_html:
Given integral is: {tex}\int_{0}^{1} x \ln (1+2 x) d x{/tex} 
Let 1 + 2x = t {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2dx = dt
At x = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} t = 1 and at x = 1 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} t = 3
{tex}\Rightarrow \int_{0}^{1} x \ln (1+2 x) d x{/tex}  = {tex}\int_{1}^{3}(t-1) \ln t d t{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\int_{1}^{3}(t-1){/tex} In t dt = {tex}\int_{1}^{3} t \ln t d t-\int_{1}^{3} \ln t d t{/tex} ......(i)
Apply Integration by parts
{tex}\Rightarrow \int t\ln\ t d t{/tex} = {tex}\ln \mathrm{t} \int \mathrm{td} \mathrm{t}-\int \frac{\mathrm{d}}{\mathrm{dt}}(\ln \mathrm{t})\left(\int \mathrm{tdt}\right) \mathrm{d} \mathrm{t}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{t^{2}}{2} \ln t-\frac{t^{2}}{4}{/tex} .....(ii)
{tex}\Rightarrow \int \ln t d t{/tex} = {tex}\ln \mathrm{t} \int \mathrm{dt}-\int \frac{\mathrm{d}}{\mathrm{dt}}(\ln \mathrm{t})\left(\int \mathrm{dt}\right) \mathrm{d} \mathrm{t}{/tex} = t In t - t ......(iii)
Put (ii) and (iii) in (i)
{tex}\Rightarrow \int_{1}^{3} t \ln t d t-\int_{1}^{3} \ln t d t{/tex} = {tex}\left[\left(\frac{t^{2}}{2} \ln t-\frac{t^{2}}{4}\right)-(t \ln t-t)\right]_{1}^{3}=\frac{3}{2} \ln 3{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \int_{0}^{1} x \ln (1+2 x) d x=\frac{3}{2} \ln 3{/tex}

x) = sin x {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2I = {tex}\int_{0}^{\pi} \pi \ln (\sin x) d x=\pi \int_{0}^{\pi} \ln (\sin x) d x{/tex} ......(i) Now in {tex}\int_{0}^{\pi} \ln (\sin x) d x{/tex} Using the property: {tex}\int_{0}^{2 a} f(x) d x{/tex} = {tex}2 \int_{0}^{a} f(x) d x{/tex} (for f(2a - x) = f(x)) {tex}\Rightarrow \int_{0}^{\pi} \ln (\sin x) d x{/tex} = {tex}2 \int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \ln (\sin x) d x{/tex} ......(ii) Let Z = {tex}\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \ln (\sin x) d x{/tex} ......(iii) Using the property {tex}\int_{a}^{b} f(x) d x=\int_{a}^{b} f(a+b-x) d x{/tex} Z = {tex}\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \ln \left(\sin \left(\frac{\pi}{2}-x\right) d x=\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \ln (\cos x) d x\right.{/tex} .....(iv) Adding equations (iii) and (iv), 2Z = {tex}\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \ln (\sin x) d x+\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \ln (\cos x) d x{/tex} = {tex}\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \ln (\sin x \cos x) d x{/tex} .....(v) {tex}\Rightarrow \int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \ln (\sin x \cos x) d x{/tex} = {tex}\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \ln \left(\frac{2 \sin x \cos x}{2}\right) d x{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \ln \left(\frac{2 \sin x \cos x}{2}\right) d x{/tex} = {tex}\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}}(\ln (\sin 2 x)-\ln 2) d x{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}}(\ln (\sin 2 x)) d x-\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}}(\ln 2) d x{/tex} = {tex}\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \ln (\sin 2 x) d x-\frac{\pi \ln 2}{2}{/tex} ....(vi) Now in {tex}\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \ln (\sin 2 x) d x{/tex} put 2x = t {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2dx = dt and limits changes from 0 to {tex}\pi{/tex} 2Z = {tex}\frac{1}{2} \int_{0}^{\pi} \ln (\sin t) d t-\frac{\pi \ln 2}{2}{/tex} From equation (ii) {tex}\frac{1}{2} \int_{0}^{\pi} \ln (\sin t) d t{/tex} again becomes, 2Z = {tex}\frac{2}{2} \int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \ln (\sin t) d t-\frac{\pi \ln 2}{2}{/tex} From equation (iii) 2Z = Z - {tex}\frac{\pi \ln 2}{2}{/tex} Z = {tex}\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \ln (\sin x) d x=-\frac{\pi \ln 2}{2}{/tex} ......(vii) On putting (vii) in (ii) and the obtained result in(i) 2I = {tex}-\pi^{2} \ln 2{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}=\int_{0}^{\pi} \mathrm{x} \ln (\sin \mathrm{x}) \mathrm{d} \mathrm{x}=-\frac{\pi^{2}}{2} \ln 2{/tex}
solution_html:
Given: {tex}\int_{0}^{\pi} x \ln (\sin x) d x{/tex} 
Using the property {tex}\int_{a}^{b} f(x) d x=\int_{a}^{b} f(a+b-x) d x{/tex} 
Let I = {tex}\int_{0}^{\pi} x \ln (\sin x) d x{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \int_{0}^{\pi}(\pi-x) \ln (\sin (\pi-x)) d x{/tex} = {tex}\int_{0}^{\pi} \pi \ln (\sin x) d x-\int_{0}^{\pi} x \ln (\sin x) d x{/tex} 
As sin ({tex}\pi{/tex} - x) = sin x
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2I = {tex}\int_{0}^{\pi} \pi \ln (\sin x) d x=\pi \int_{0}^{\pi} \ln (\sin x) d x{/tex} ......(i)
Now in {tex}\int_{0}^{\pi} \ln (\sin x) d x{/tex} 
Using the property: {tex}\int_{0}^{2 a} f(x) d x{/tex} = {tex}2 \int_{0}^{a} f(x) d x{/tex} (for f(2a - x) = f(x))
{tex}\Rightarrow \int_{0}^{\pi} \ln (\sin x) d x{/tex} = {tex}2 \int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \ln (\sin x) d x{/tex} ......(ii)
Let Z = {tex}\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \ln (\sin x) d x{/tex} ......(iii)
Using the property {tex}\int_{a}^{b} f(x) d x=\int_{a}^{b} f(a+b-x) d x{/tex} 
Z = {tex}\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \ln \left(\sin \left(\frac{\pi}{2}-x\right) d x=\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \ln (\cos x) d x\right.{/tex} .....(iv)
Adding equations (iii) and (iv),
2Z = {tex}\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \ln (\sin x) d x+\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \ln (\cos x) d x{/tex} = {tex}\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \ln (\sin x \cos x) d x{/tex} .....(v)
{tex}\Rightarrow \int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \ln (\sin x \cos x) d x{/tex} = {tex}\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \ln \left(\frac{2 \sin x \cos x}{2}\right) d x{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \ln \left(\frac{2 \sin x \cos x}{2}\right) d x{/tex} = {tex}\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}}(\ln (\sin 2 x)-\ln 2) d x{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}}(\ln (\sin 2 x)) d x-\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}}(\ln 2) d x{/tex} = {tex}\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \ln (\sin 2 x) d x-\frac{\pi \ln 2}{2}{/tex} ....(vi)
Now in {tex}\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \ln (\sin 2 x) d x{/tex}  put 2x = t
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2dx = dt and limits changes from 0 to {tex}\pi{/tex} 
2Z = {tex}\frac{1}{2} \int_{0}^{\pi} \ln (\sin t) d t-\frac{\pi \ln 2}{2}{/tex} 
From equation (ii) {tex}\frac{1}{2} \int_{0}^{\pi} \ln (\sin t) d t{/tex} again becomes,
2Z = {tex}\frac{2}{2} \int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \ln (\sin t) d t-\frac{\pi \ln 2}{2}{/tex} 
From equation (iii)
2Z = Z - {tex}\frac{\pi \ln 2}{2}{/tex} 
Z = {tex}\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \ln (\sin x) d x=-\frac{\pi \ln 2}{2}{/tex} ......(vii)
On putting (vii) in (ii) and the obtained result in(i)
2I = {tex}-\pi^{2} \ln 2{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}=\int_{0}^{\pi} \mathrm{x} \ln (\sin \mathrm{x}) \mathrm{d} \mathrm{x}=-\frac{\pi^{2}}{2} \ln 2{/tex}
