solution_text: Let I = {tex}\int_{-\frac{\pi}{4}}^{\frac{\pi}{4}} \ln (\sin x+\cos x) d x{/tex} ....(i) Using the property: {tex}\int_{a}^{b} f(x) d x=\int_{a}^{b} f(a+b-x) d x{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \int_{-\frac{\pi}{4}}^{\frac{\pi}{4}} \ln (\sin x+\cos x) d x{/tex} = {tex}\int_{-\frac{\pi}{4}}^{\frac{\pi}{4}} \ln (\sin (-x)+\cos (-x)) d x{/tex} I = {tex}\int_{-\frac{\pi}{4}}^{\frac{\pi}{4}} \ln (\cos x-\sin x) d x{/tex} .......(ii) Adding equation (i) and (ii) 2I = {tex}\int_{-\frac{\pi}{4}}^{\frac{\pi}{4}} \ln (\sin x+\cos x) d x{/tex} + {tex}\int_{-\frac{\pi}{4}}^{\frac{\pi}{4}} \ln (\cos x-\sin x) d x{/tex} = {tex}\int_{-\frac{\pi}{4}}^{\frac{\pi}{4}} \ln \left(\cos ^{2} x-\sin ^{2} x\right) d x{/tex} = {tex}\int_{-\frac{\pi}{4}}^{\frac{\pi}{4}} \ln (\cos 2 x) d x{/tex} As cos(-x) = cos x Using property: {tex}\int_{-a}^{a} f(x) d x= 2\int_{0}^{a} f(x) d x{/tex} (for f(-x) = f(x)) {tex}\Rightarrow 2 I=2 \int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{4}} \log \cos 2 x d x{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow I=\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{4}} \log \cos 2 x d x{/tex} .... (iii) Now, by property {tex}\int_{0}^{a} f(a-x) d x= \int_{0}^{a} f(x) d x{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow I=\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{4}} \log \left(\cos 2\left[\frac{\pi}{4}-x\right]\right) d x=\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{4}} \log \left(\cos \frac{\pi}{2}-2 x\right) d x{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow I=\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{4}} \log \sin 2 x d x{/tex} .... (iv) Adding Equations, (iii) and (iv), we get {tex}\Rightarrow 2 I=\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{4}}(\log \sin 2 x+\log \cos 2 x d x){/tex} {tex}=\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{4}} \log (\sin 2 x \cos 2 x) d x{/tex} {tex}=\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{4}} \log \left(\frac{1}{2} \sin 4 x\right) d x{/tex} {tex}=\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{4}} \log \sin 4 x d x+\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{4}} \log \frac{1}{2} d x{/tex} {tex}=\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{4}} \log \sin 4 x d x+[x]_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{4}} \log \frac{1}{2}{/tex} {tex}= \int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{4}} \log \sin 4 x d x+\frac{\pi}{4} \log \frac{1}{2}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow 2 I=I_{1}+\frac{\pi}{4} \log \frac{1}{2}{/tex} Where, {tex}I_{1} = \int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{4}} \log \sin 4 x d x{/tex} Let 2x = t {tex}\Rightarrow 2dx = dt{/tex} When x = 0, t = 0 and at {tex}x=\frac{\pi}{4}, t=\frac{\pi}{2}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow I_{1}=\frac{1}{2} \int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \log \sin 2 t d t{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow I_{1}=\frac{1}{2} 2 \int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{4}} \log \sin 2 t d t{/tex} [Using {tex}\int_{0}^{2 a} f(x) d x=2 \int_{0}^{a} f(x) d x{/tex} ] {tex}=\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{4}} \log \sin 2 t d t{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow I_{1}=\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{4}} \log \sin 2 x d x{/tex} ...(by change of variable property) Hence, {tex}2 I=\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{4}} \log \sin 2 x d x+\frac{\pi}{4} \log \frac{1}{2}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow 2 I=I+\frac{\pi}{4} \log \frac{1}{2}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow I=\frac{\pi}{4} \log \frac{1}{2}{/tex}
solution_html:
Let I = {tex}\int_{-\frac{\pi}{4}}^{\frac{\pi}{4}} \ln (\sin x+\cos x) d x{/tex} ....(i)
Using the property: {tex}\int_{a}^{b} f(x) d x=\int_{a}^{b} f(a+b-x) d x{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \int_{-\frac{\pi}{4}}^{\frac{\pi}{4}} \ln (\sin x+\cos x) d x{/tex} = {tex}\int_{-\frac{\pi}{4}}^{\frac{\pi}{4}} \ln (\sin (-x)+\cos (-x)) d x{/tex} 
I = {tex}\int_{-\frac{\pi}{4}}^{\frac{\pi}{4}} \ln (\cos x-\sin x) d x{/tex} .......(ii)
Adding equation (i) and (ii)
2I = {tex}\int_{-\frac{\pi}{4}}^{\frac{\pi}{4}} \ln (\sin x+\cos x) d x{/tex} + {tex}\int_{-\frac{\pi}{4}}^{\frac{\pi}{4}} \ln (\cos x-\sin x) d x{/tex} 
= {tex}\int_{-\frac{\pi}{4}}^{\frac{\pi}{4}} \ln \left(\cos ^{2} x-\sin ^{2} x\right) d x{/tex} 
= {tex}\int_{-\frac{\pi}{4}}^{\frac{\pi}{4}} \ln (\cos 2 x) d x{/tex} 
As cos(-x) = cos x
Using property: {tex}\int_{-a}^{a} f(x) d x= 2\int_{0}^{a} f(x) d x{/tex}  (for f(-x) = f(x))
{tex}\Rightarrow 2 I=2 \int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{4}} \log \cos 2 x d x{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow I=\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{4}} \log \cos 2 x d x{/tex} .... (iii)
Now, by property {tex}\int_{0}^{a} f(a-x) d x= \int_{0}^{a} f(x) d x{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow I=\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{4}} \log \left(\cos 2\left[\frac{\pi}{4}-x\right]\right) d x=\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{4}} \log \left(\cos \frac{\pi}{2}-2 x\right) d x{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow I=\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{4}} \log \sin 2 x d x{/tex} .... (iv)
Adding Equations, (iii) and (iv), we get
{tex}\Rightarrow 2 I=\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{4}}(\log \sin 2 x+\log \cos 2 x d x){/tex}
{tex}=\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{4}} \log (\sin 2 x \cos 2 x) d x{/tex}
{tex}=\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{4}} \log \left(\frac{1}{2} \sin 4 x\right) d x{/tex}
{tex}=\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{4}} \log \sin 4 x d x+\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{4}} \log \frac{1}{2} d x{/tex}
{tex}=\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{4}} \log \sin 4 x d x+[x]_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{4}} \log \frac{1}{2}{/tex}
{tex}= \int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{4}} \log \sin 4 x d x+\frac{\pi}{4} \log \frac{1}{2}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow 2 I=I_{1}+\frac{\pi}{4} \log \frac{1}{2}{/tex}
Where, {tex}I_{1} = \int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{4}} \log \sin 4 x d x{/tex}
Let 2x = t
{tex}\Rightarrow 2dx = dt{/tex}
When x = 0, t = 0 and at {tex}x=\frac{\pi}{4}, t=\frac{\pi}{2}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow I_{1}=\frac{1}{2} \int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \log \sin 2 t d t{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow I_{1}=\frac{1}{2} 2 \int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{4}} \log \sin 2 t d t{/tex}   [Using  {tex}\int_{0}^{2 a} f(x) d x=2 \int_{0}^{a} f(x) d x{/tex}]
{tex}=\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{4}} \log \sin 2 t d t{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow I_{1}=\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{4}} \log \sin 2 x d x{/tex}   ...(by change of variable property)
Hence, {tex}2 I=\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{4}} \log \sin 2 x d x+\frac{\pi}{4} \log \frac{1}{2}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow 2 I=I+\frac{\pi}{4} \log \frac{1}{2}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow I=\frac{\pi}{4} \log \frac{1}{2}{/tex}

question_text: {tex}\int \frac{\cos 2 x-\cos 2 \theta}{\cos x-\cos \theta} d x{/tex} is equal to
question_html: {tex}\int \frac{\cos 2 x-\cos 2 \theta}{\cos x-\cos \theta} d x{/tex} is equal to

options.explanation: Using Trigonometric identity cos 2x = 2 cos 2 x - 1 {tex}\Rightarrow \int \frac{\cos 2 x-\cos 2 \theta}{\cos x-\cos \theta} d x{/tex} = {tex}\int \frac{\left(2 \cos ^{2} x-1\right)-\left(2 \cos ^{2} \theta-1\right)}{\cos x-\cos \theta} d x{/tex} = {tex}2 \int \frac{\cos ^{2} x-\cos ^{2} \theta}{\cos x-\cos \theta} d x{/tex} = {tex}2 \int\left(\frac{(\cos x+\cos \theta)(\cos x-\cos \theta)}{\cos x-\cos \theta}\right){/tex} =2 {tex}\int{/tex} (cos x + cos {tex}\theta{/tex} )dx =2 {tex}\int{/tex} cos x dx + 2 {tex}\int{/tex} cos {tex}\theta{/tex} dx {tex}\Rightarrow 2 \int \cos x d x+2 \int \cos \theta d x=2(\sin x+x \cos \theta)+c{/tex}

options.explanation: Given integral is: {tex}\int \frac{d x}{\sin (x-a) \sin (x-b)}{/tex} Multiply Nr. and Dr. by sin (b - a) {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{1}{\sin (b-a)} \int \frac{\sin (b-a) d x}{\sin (x-a) \sin (x-b)}{/tex} Now, sin (b - a) = sin ((x - a) - (x - b)) Also, sin (A - B) = sin A cos B - cos A sin B {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{\sin (b-a)}{\sin (x-a) \sin (x-b)}=\frac{\sin ((x-a)-(x-b))}{\sin (x-a) \sin (x-b)}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{\sin (x-a) \cos (x-b)-\cos (x-a) \sin (x-b)}{\sin (x-a) \sin (x-b)}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{\sin (x-a) \cos (x-b)-\cos (x-a) \sin (x-b)}{\sin (x-a) \sin (x-b)}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{\cos (x-b)}{\sin (x-b)}-\frac{\cos (x-a)}{\sin (x-a)}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{\cos (x-b)}{\sin (x-b)}-\frac{\cos (x-a)}{\sin (x-a)}{/tex} = cot (x - b) - cot(x - a) {tex}\Rightarrow \int \frac{d x}{\sin (x-a) \sin (x-b)}=\frac{1}{\sin (b-a)}{/tex} ( {tex}\int \cot (x-b) d x-\int \cot (x-a) d x{/tex} ) Now {tex}\int \cot x d x=\ln |\sin x|+C{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \int \frac{d x}{\sin (x-a) \sin (x-b)}= \frac{1}{\sin (b-a)}\left(\int \cot (x-b) d x-\int \cot (x-a) d x\right){/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{\sin (b-a)}(\ln |\sin (x-b)|-\ln |\sin (x-a)|){/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \int \frac{d x}{\sin (x-a) \sin (x-b)}=\frac{1}{\sin (b-a)} \ln \left(\frac{\sin (x-b)}{\sin (x-a)}\right){/tex} = {tex}cosec (b-a) \ln \left(\frac{\sin (x-b)}{\sin (x-a)}\right){/tex}

options.explanation: Given integral is {tex}\int \tan ^{-1} \sqrt{x} ~d x{/tex} Put x = t 2 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} dx = 2tdt {tex}\Rightarrow \int \tan ^{-1} \sqrt{x} d x=\int 2 t\tan ^{-1} \sqrt{t^{2}} d t{/tex} {tex}=2 \int t \tan ^{-1} t d t{/tex} .....(i) Now apply integration by part on {tex}\int t \tan ^{-1} t d t{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \int t \tan ^{-1} t d t{/tex} = {tex}\tan ^{-1} \mathrm{t} \int \mathrm{td} \mathrm{t}-\int\left(\frac{\mathrm{d}}{\mathrm{dt}} \tan ^{-1} \mathrm{t}\right)\left(\int \mathrm{td} \mathrm{t}\right) \mathrm{d} \mathrm{t}{/tex} {tex}= \frac{t^{2}}{2} \tan ^{-1} t-\frac{1}{2} \int \frac{t^{2}}{1+t^{2}} d t{/tex} ......(ii) Next, {tex}\int \frac{t^{2}}{1+t^{2}} d t=\int \frac{t^{2}+1-1}{1+t^{2}} d t{/tex} = {tex}\int \frac{t^{2}+1}{1+t^{2}} d t-\int \frac{1}{1+t^{2}} d t{/tex} {tex}= \int \frac{t^{2}+1}{1+t^{2}} d t-\int \frac{1}{1+t^{2}} d t{/tex} = {tex}\int \mathrm{dt}-\int \frac{1}{1+\mathrm{t}^{2}} \mathrm{dt}{/tex} .....(iii) {tex}=\mathrm{t}-\tan ^{-1} \mathrm{t}{/tex} Put (iii) in (ii) and then resulting equation in (i), we get {tex}\Rightarrow 2 \int t \tan ^{-1} t d t{/tex} = {tex}2\left(\frac{t^{2}}{2} \tan ^{-1} t-\frac{1}{2}\left(t-\tan ^{-1} t\right)\right){/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow 2 \int t \tan ^{-1} t d t{/tex} = {tex}t^2 tan^{-1} t - t + tan^{-1} t{/tex} = {tex}t^2 tan^{-1} t - t + tan^{-1} t {/tex} {tex}= (t^2 + 1) tan^{-1} t- t{/tex} {tex}=(x + 1) tan^{-1} \sqrt x -\sqrt x {/tex} . Which is the required solution.

question_text: {tex}\int e^{x}\left(\frac{1-x}{1+x^{2}}\right)^{2} d x{/tex} is equal to
question_html: {tex}\int e^{x}\left(\frac{1-x}{1+x^{2}}\right)^{2} d x{/tex} is equal to

options.explanation: Given {tex}\int e^{x}\left(\frac{1-x}{1+x^{2}}\right)^{2} d x{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \int e^{x}\left(\frac{1-x}{1+x^{2}}\right)^{2} d x{/tex} = {tex}\int e^{x}\left(\frac{1+x^{2}-2 x}{\left(1+x^{2}\right)^{2}}\right) d x{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \int e^{x}\left(\frac{1+x^{2}-2 x}{\left(1+x^{2}\right)^{2}}\right) d x{/tex} = {tex}\int e^{x}\left\{\left(\frac{1+x^{2}}{\left(1+x^{2}\right)^{2}}\right)+\left(\frac{-2 x}{\left(1+x^{2}\right)^{2}}\right)\right\} d x{/tex} = {tex}\int e^{x}\left\{\left(\frac{1}{\left(1+x^{2}\right)}\right)+\left(\frac{-2 x}{\left(1+x^{2}\right)^{2}}\right)\right\} d x{/tex} Now using the property: {tex}\int \mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{x}}\left(\mathrm{f}(\mathrm{x})+\mathrm{f}^{\prime}(\mathrm{x})\right) \mathrm{d} \mathrm{x}=\mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{x}} \mathrm{f}(\mathrm{x}){/tex} Now in {tex}\int e^{x}\left\{\left(\frac{1}{\left(1+x^{2}\right)}\right)+\left(\frac{-2 x}{\left(1+x^{2}\right)^{2}}\right)\right\} d x{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} f(x) = {tex}\frac{1}{\left(1+x^{2}\right)}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} f'(x) = {tex}\frac{-2 x}{\left(1+x^{2}\right)^{2}}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \int \mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{x}}\left\{\left(\frac{1}{\left(1+\mathrm{x}^{2}\right)}\right)+\left(\frac{-2 \mathrm{x}}{\left(1+\mathrm{x}^{2}\right)^{2}}\right)\right\} \mathrm{d} \mathrm{x}=\frac{\mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{x}}}{1+\mathrm{x}^{2}}+\mathrm{C}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \int e^{x}\left(\frac{1-x}{1+x^{2}}\right)^{2} d x=\frac{e^{x}}{1+x^{2}}+C{/tex} . Which is the required solution.

options.explanation: Given: {tex}\int\left(\frac{x^{9}}{\left(4 x^{2}+1\right)^{6}}\right) d x{/tex} Taking x 1 2 out from the denominator {tex}\Rightarrow \int\left(\frac{x^{9}}{x^{12}\left(4+\frac{1}{x^{2}}\right)^{6}}\right) d x{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \int\left(\frac{1}{x^{3}\left(4+\frac{1}{x^{2}}\right)^{6}}\right) d x{/tex} = {tex}\int\left(\frac{\frac{1}{x^{3}}}{\left(4+\frac{1}{x^{2}}\right)^{6}}\right) d x{/tex} Now put {tex}4+\frac{1}{x^{2}}=t{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow-\frac{1}{x^{3}} d x{/tex} = dt {tex}\Rightarrow \int\left(\frac{\frac{1}{x^{3}}}{\left(4+\frac{1}{x^{2}}\right)^{6}}\right) d x{/tex} = {tex}\int-\frac{1}{t^{6}} d t{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \int-\mathrm{t}^{-6} \mathrm{dt}=\frac{-\mathrm{t}^{-5}}{-5}+\mathrm{C}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{5}\left(4+\frac{1}{x^{2}}\right)^{-5}+C{/tex}

options.explanation: Given {tex}\int_{-\frac{\pi}{4}}^{\frac{\pi}{4}}\left(\frac{1}{1+\cos 2 x}\right)dx{/tex} Using trigonometric identities: cos 2 x + sin 2 x = 1 and cos 2x = cos 2 x - sin 2 x, we get {tex}\frac{1}{1+\cos 2 x}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{\left(\cos ^{2} x+\sin ^{2} x+\cos ^{2} x-\sin ^{2} x\right)}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{2 \cos ^{2} x}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\int_{-\frac{\pi}{4}}^{\frac{\pi}{4}}\left(\frac{1}{1+\cos 2 x}\right)dx{/tex} = {tex}\int_{-\frac{\pi}{4}}^{\frac{\pi}{4}}\left(\frac{d x}{2 \cos ^{2} x}\right){/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{2} \int_{-\frac{\pi}{4}}^{\frac{\pi}{4}} \sec ^{2} x d x{/tex} {tex}=\frac{1}{2} \int_{-\frac{\pi}{4}}^{\frac{\pi}{4}} \sec ^{2} x d x=\frac{1}{2}[\tan x]_{-\frac{\pi}{4}}^{\frac{\pi}{4}}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1+1}{2}{/tex} = 1

As sin 2x = 2 sin x cos x and sin 2 x + cos 2 x = 1 {tex}\Rightarrow \int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \sqrt{1-\sin 2 x} d x=\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \sqrt{\sin ^{2} x+\cos ^{2} x-2 \sin x \cos x} d x{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \sqrt{(\cos x-\sin x)^{2}} d x{/tex} = {tex}\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}}|(\cos x-\sin x)| d x{/tex} From {tex}0<x<\frac{\pi}{4}{/tex} , cos x > sin x and from {tex}\frac{\pi}{4}<x<\frac{\pi}{2}{/tex} , cos x < sin x {tex}\Rightarrow \int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}}|(\cos x-\sin x)| d x{/tex} = {tex}\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{4}}(\cos x-\sin x) d x+\int_{\frac{\pi}{4}}^{\frac{\pi}{2}}(\sin x-\cos x) d x{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} On solving the Above Integral we get 2( {tex}\sqrt{2}{/tex} - 1)

question_html: Evaluate {tex}\int \frac{3 a x}{b^{2}+c^{2} x^{2}} d x{/tex}
solution_text: Let v = b 2 + c 2 x 2 , then dv = 2c 2 xdx Therefore, {tex}\int \frac{3 a x}{b^{2}+c^{2} x^{2}} d x=\frac{3 a}{2 c^{2}} \int \frac{d v}{v}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{3 a}{2 c^{2}} \log \left|b^{2}+c^{2} x^{2}\right|+C{/tex}
solution_html:
Let v = b2 + c2x2, then dv = 2c2 xdx
Therefore, {tex}\int \frac{3 a x}{b^{2}+c^{2} x^{2}} d x=\frac{3 a}{2 c^{2}} \int \frac{d v}{v}{/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{3 a}{2 c^{2}} \log \left|b^{2}+c^{2} x^{2}\right|+C{/tex}

question_text: Verify the following using the concept of integration as an antiderivative. {tex}\int {\frac{{{x^3}dx}}{{x + 1}} = x - \frac{{{x^2}}}{2} + \frac{{{x^3}}}{3} - \log \left| {x + 1} \right| + C} {/tex}
question_html:
Verify the following using the concept of integration as an antiderivative.

{tex}\int {\frac{{{x^3}dx}}{{x + 1}} = x - \frac{{{x^2}}}{2} + \frac{{{x^3}}}{3} - \log \left| {x + 1} \right| + C} {/tex}
solution_html:
{tex}\frac{d}{{dx}} \left( {x - \frac{{{x^2}}}{2} + \frac{{{x^3}}}{3} - \log \left| {x + 1} \right| + C} \right){/tex}

{tex} = 1 - \frac{{2x}}{2} + \frac{{3{x^2}}}{3} - \frac{1}{{x + 1}}{/tex}

{tex} = 1 - x + {x^2} - \frac{1}{{x +1}} \\ =\frac{(x+1)(1-x+x^2)-1}{x+1}=\frac{x-x^2+x^3+1-x+x^2-1}{x+1}=\frac{x^3}{x+1} {/tex}

Thus {tex}\left( {x - \frac{{{x^2}}}{2} + \frac{{{x^3}}}{3} - \log \left| {x + 1} \right| + C} \right) = \int {\frac{{{x^3}}}{{x + 1}}dx} {/tex}

solution_text: {tex} I=\int {\sqrt {\frac{{1 + x}}{{1 - x}}} dx }\\ = \int {\sqrt {\frac{{(1 + x)(1+x)}}{{(1 - x)(1+x)}}} dx } {/tex} {tex} =\int {{\frac{{1 + x}}{{\sqrt{1 - x^2}}}} dx } {/tex} {tex}{ = \int {\frac{1}{{\sqrt {1 - {x^2}} }}dx} + \int {\frac{{xdx}}{{\sqrt {1 - {x^2}} }} = {{\sin }^{ - 1}}x + {I_1},} }{/tex} where {tex}{I_1} = \int {\frac{{xdx}}{{\sqrt {1 - {x^2}} }}} {/tex} Put {tex}1 - {x^2} = {t^2} \Rightarrow - 2xdx = 2tdt.{/tex} Therefore {tex}{I_1} = - \int {dt = - t + C = - \sqrt {1 - {x^2}} + C} {/tex} Hence {tex}I = {\sin ^{ - 1}}x - \sqrt {1 - {x^2}} + C.{/tex}
solution_html:
{tex} I=\int {\sqrt {\frac{{1 + x}}{{1 - x}}} dx }\\ = \int {\sqrt {\frac{{(1 + x)(1+x)}}{{(1 - x)(1+x)}}} dx } {/tex}

{tex} =\int {{\frac{{1 + x}}{{\sqrt{1 - x^2}}}} dx } {/tex}

{tex}{ = \int {\frac{1}{{\sqrt {1 - {x^2}} }}dx} + \int {\frac{{xdx}}{{\sqrt {1 - {x^2}} }} = {{\sin }^{ - 1}}x + {I_1},} }{/tex}

where {tex}{I_1} = \int {\frac{{xdx}}{{\sqrt {1 - {x^2}} }}} {/tex}

Put {tex}1 - {x^2} = {t^2} \Rightarrow - 2xdx = 2tdt.{/tex}Therefore

{tex}{I_1} = - \int {dt = - t + C = - \sqrt {1 - {x^2}} + C} {/tex}

Hence {tex}I = {\sin ^{ - 1}}x - \sqrt {1 - {x^2}} + C.{/tex}

solution_text: Let, {tex}I =\int {\frac{{dx}}{{\sqrt {\left( {x - \alpha } \right)\left( {\beta - x} \right)} }},\beta > \alpha } {/tex} Put {tex}x - \alpha = {t^2}{/tex} . Then {tex}\beta - x = \beta - \left( {{t^2} + \alpha } \right) {/tex} {tex}= \beta - {t^2} - \alpha = - {t^2} - \alpha + \beta{/tex} and dx = 2tdt. Now {tex}I = \int {\frac{{2t\,dt}}{{\sqrt {{t^2}\left( {\beta - \alpha - {t^2}} \right)} }} = \int {\frac{{2dt}}{{\sqrt {\left( {\beta - \alpha - {t^2}} \right)} }}} } {/tex} {tex} = 2\int {\frac{{dt}}{{\sqrt {{k^2} - {t^2}} }},} {/tex} where {tex}{k^2} = \beta - \alpha{/tex} {tex}= 2{\sin ^{ - 1}}\frac{t}{k} + C {/tex} {tex}= 2{\sin ^{ - 1}}\sqrt {\frac{{x - \alpha }}{{\beta - \alpha }}} + C{/tex}
solution_html:
Let, {tex}I =\int {\frac{{dx}}{{\sqrt {\left( {x - \alpha } \right)\left( {\beta - x} \right)} }},\beta > \alpha } {/tex}
Put {tex}x - \alpha = {t^2}{/tex}.
Then {tex}\beta - x = \beta - \left( {{t^2} + \alpha } \right) {/tex} {tex}= \beta - {t^2} - \alpha = - {t^2} - \alpha + \beta{/tex} and dx = 2tdt. 
Now
{tex}I = \int {\frac{{2t\,dt}}{{\sqrt {{t^2}\left( {\beta - \alpha - {t^2}} \right)} }} = \int {\frac{{2dt}}{{\sqrt {\left( {\beta - \alpha - {t^2}} \right)} }}} } {/tex}
{tex} = 2\int {\frac{{dt}}{{\sqrt {{k^2} - {t^2}} }},} {/tex} where {tex}{k^2} = \beta - \alpha{/tex}
{tex}= 2{\sin ^{ - 1}}\frac{t}{k} + C {/tex}
{tex}= 2{\sin ^{ - 1}}\sqrt {\frac{{x - \alpha }}{{\beta - \alpha }}} + C{/tex}

solution_html:
I = {tex}\int \tan ^{8} x \sec ^{4} x d x{/tex} 
= {tex}\int \tan ^{8} x\left(\sec ^{2} x\right) \sec ^{2} x d x{/tex} 
= {tex}\int \tan ^{8} x\left(\tan ^{2} x+1\right) \sec ^{2} x d x{/tex}
= {tex}\int \tan ^{10} x \sec ^{2} x d x+\int \tan ^{8} x \sec ^{2} x d x{/tex} 
Let t = tan x {tex}\Rightarrow\mathrm{dt}=\sec ^{2} \mathrm{xdx}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow I=\int\left(t^{10}+t^{8}\right) d t{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{t^{11}}{11}+\frac{t^{9}}{9}+C{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{\tan ^{11} x}{11}+\frac{\tan ^{9} x}{9}+C{/tex}

question_text: Find {tex}\int \frac { x ^ { 3 } } { x ^ { 4 } + 3 x ^ { 2 } + 2 } d x.{/tex}
question_html: Find {tex}\int \frac { x ^ { 3 } } { x ^ { 4 } + 3 x ^ { 2 } + 2 } d x.{/tex}
solution_text: According to the question, {tex}I = \int \frac { x ^ { 3 } } { x ^ { 4 } + 3 x ^ { 2 } + 2 } d x{/tex} {tex}I = \int \frac { x ^ { 2 }.x } { x ^ { 4 } + 3 x ^ { 2 } + 2 } d x{/tex} Let {tex}x ^ { 2 } = t \Rightarrow 2 x dx= { d t }{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \quad x d x = \frac { d t } { 2 }{/tex} {tex}\therefore \quad I = \frac { 1 } { 2 } \int \frac { t } { t ^ { 2 } + 3 t + 2 } d t {/tex} {tex}= \frac { 1 } { 2 } \int \frac { t } { ( t + 2 )( t + 1 ) } d t{/tex} By using partial fractions, {tex} \frac { t } { ( t + 2 )( t + 1 ) } = \frac {A}{t+2} +\frac {B}{t+1}{/tex} {tex}I= \frac { 1 } { 2 } \int \frac { t } { ( t + 2 )( t + 1 ) } d t = \frac { 1 } { 2 } \int \frac {A}{t+2} +\frac {B}{t+1} dt{/tex} .(i) {tex}t = A(t+1)+B(t+2){/tex} if {tex}\ \ t = -2\Rightarrow -2=A(-1) {/tex} {tex}, \therefore A=2{/tex} if {tex}\ \ t = -1\Rightarrow -1=B(1) , \therefore B=-1{/tex} put values of A and B in (i) {tex}I= \frac { 1 } { 2 } \left[ \int \frac { 2 } { t + 2 } d t - \int \frac { 1 } { t + 1 } d t \right]{/tex} {tex}= \frac { 1 } { 2 } [ 2 \log | t + 2 | - \log | t + 1 | ] + C{/tex} {tex}= \log | t + 2 | - \frac { 1 } { 2 } \log | t + 1 | + C{/tex} {tex}= \log | t + 2 | - \log \sqrt { t + 1 } + C{/tex} {tex}= \log \left| \frac { t + 2 } { \sqrt { t + 1 } } \right| + C {/tex} {tex}put\ t = x^2{/tex} {tex}I= \log \left| \frac { x ^ { 2 } + 2 } { \sqrt { x ^ { 2 } + 1 } } \right| + C{/tex}
solution_html:
According to the question,  {tex}I = \int \frac { x ^ { 3 } } { x ^ { 4 } + 3 x ^ { 2 } + 2 } d x{/tex}

{tex}I = \int \frac { x ^ { 2 }.x } { x ^ { 4 } + 3 x ^ { 2 } + 2 } d x{/tex}
Let {tex}x ^ { 2 } = t \Rightarrow 2 x dx= { d t }{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad x d x = \frac { d t } { 2 }{/tex}
{tex}\therefore \quad I = \frac { 1 } { 2 } \int \frac { t } { t ^ { 2 } + 3 t + 2 } d t {/tex}

{tex}= \frac { 1 } { 2 } \int \frac { t } { ( t + 2 )( t + 1 ) } d t{/tex}

By using partial fractions,

{tex} \frac { t } { ( t + 2 )( t + 1 ) } = \frac {A}{t+2} +\frac {B}{t+1}{/tex}

{tex}I= \frac { 1 } { 2 } \int \frac { t } { ( t + 2 )( t + 1 ) } d t = \frac { 1 } { 2 } \int \frac {A}{t+2} +\frac {B}{t+1} dt{/tex}    .(i)

{tex}t = A(t+1)+B(t+2){/tex}

if  {tex}\ \ t = -2\Rightarrow -2=A(-1) {/tex}   {tex}, \therefore A=2{/tex}

if {tex}\ \ t = -1\Rightarrow -1=B(1) , \therefore B=-1{/tex}

put values of A and B in (i)
{tex}I= \frac { 1 } { 2 } \left[ \int \frac { 2 } { t + 2 } d t - \int \frac { 1 } { t + 1 } d t \right]{/tex}
{tex}= \frac { 1 } { 2 } [ 2 \log | t + 2 | - \log | t + 1 | ] + C{/tex}
{tex}= \log | t + 2 | - \frac { 1 } { 2 } \log | t + 1 | + C{/tex}
{tex}= \log | t + 2 | - \log \sqrt { t + 1 } + C{/tex}
{tex}= \log \left| \frac { t + 2 } { \sqrt { t + 1 } } \right| + C {/tex}

{tex}put\ t = x^2{/tex}

{tex}I= \log \left| \frac { x ^ { 2 } + 2 } { \sqrt { x ^ { 2 } + 1 } } \right| + C{/tex}

Then {tex}I = \int {\frac{{dt}}{{2{t^2} + 5}}} {/tex} {tex}= \frac{1}{2}\int {\frac{{dt}}{{{t^2}+{{\left( {\sqrt {\frac{5}{2}} } \right)}^2}}}}{/tex} {tex}= \frac{1}{2}\left( {\sqrt {\frac{2}{5}} } \right){\tan ^{ - 1}}\left( {\frac{{t\sqrt 2 }}{{\sqrt 5 }}} \right) + C = \frac{1}{{\sqrt {10} }}{\tan ^{ - 1}}\left( {\frac{{\sqrt 2 \tan x}}{{\sqrt 5 }}} \right) + C{/tex}
solution_html:
Dividing numerator and denominator by {tex}{\cos ^2}x{/tex} we have

{tex}I = \int {\frac{{{{\sec }^2}xdx}}{{2{{\tan }^2}x + 5}}} {/tex}

Put tan x = t so that sec2xdx = dt. Then

{tex}I = \int {\frac{{dt}}{{2{t^2} + 5}}} {/tex}

{tex}= \frac{1}{2}\int {\frac{{dt}}{{{t^2}+{{\left( {\sqrt {\frac{5}{2}} } \right)}^2}}}}{/tex}

{tex}= \frac{1}{2}\left( {\sqrt {\frac{2}{5}} } \right){\tan ^{ - 1}}\left( {\frac{{t\sqrt 2 }}{{\sqrt 5 }}} \right) + C = \frac{1}{{\sqrt {10} }}{\tan ^{ - 1}}\left( {\frac{{\sqrt 2 \tan x}}{{\sqrt 5 }}} \right) + C{/tex}

question_text: Evaluate {tex}\int_{-1}^{2}(7 x-5) d x{/tex} as a limit of sums.
question_html: Evaluate {tex}\int_{-1}^{2}(7 x-5) d x{/tex} as a limit of sums.
solution_text: Given integral is, {tex}\int_{-1}^{2}(7 x-5) d x{/tex} Here a = -1 , b = 2, and h = {tex}\frac{2+1}{n}{/tex} , i.e. , nh = 3 and f (x) = 7x - 5. Now, we have {tex}\int_{-1}^{2}(7 x-5) d x{/tex} = {tex}\mathop {{\text{lim}}}\limits_{{\text{h}} \to {\text{0}}} {\text{h}}{/tex} [f(-1) + f(-1 + h) + f(-1 + 2h) + .....+ f(-1 + (n - 1)h)] Note that f (-1) = -7 - 5 = -12 f (-1 + h) = -7 + 7h - 5 = -12 + 7h f (-1 + (n -1) h) = 7 (n - 1) h - 12. Therefore, {tex}\int_{-1}^{2}(7 x-5) d x{/tex} = {tex}\mathop {{\text{lim}}}\limits_{{\text{h}} \to {\text{0}}} {\text{h}}{/tex} [(-12) + (-7h - 12) + (14h - 12) + ..... + (7(n - 1)h - 12)] = {tex}\mathop {{\text{lim}}}\limits_{{\text{h}} \to {\text{0}}} {\text{h}}{/tex} [7h[1 + 2 + ......+ (n - 1)] - 12n] = {tex}\mathop {{\text{lim}}}\limits_{{\text{h}} \to {\text{0}}} {\text{h}}{/tex} {tex}\left[7 h \frac{(n-1) n}{2}-.12 n\right]{/tex} = {tex}\mathop {{\text{lim}}}\limits_{{\text{h}} \to {\text{0}}} {\text{h}}{/tex} {tex}\left[\frac{7}{2}(n h)(n h-h)-12 n h\right]{/tex} = {tex}\frac{7}{2}(3)(3-0)-12 \times 3{/tex} = {tex}\frac{7 \times 9}{2}-36=\frac{-9}{2}{/tex}
solution_html:
Given integral is, {tex}\int_{-1}^{2}(7 x-5) d x{/tex}
Here a = -1 , b = 2, and h = {tex}\frac{2+1}{n}{/tex}, i.e. , nh = 3 and f (x) = 7x - 5.
Now, we have
{tex}\int_{-1}^{2}(7 x-5) d x{/tex} = {tex}\mathop {{\text{lim}}}\limits_{{\text{h}} \to {\text{0}}} {\text{h}}{/tex} [f(-1) + f(-1 + h) + f(-1 + 2h) + .....+ f(-1 + (n - 1)h)]
Note that 
f (-1) = -7 - 5 = -12
f (-1 + h) = -7 + 7h - 5 = -12 + 7h
f (-1 + (n -1) h) = 7 (n - 1) h - 12.
Therefore, 
{tex}\int_{-1}^{2}(7 x-5) d x{/tex} = {tex}\mathop {{\text{lim}}}\limits_{{\text{h}} \to {\text{0}}} {\text{h}}{/tex}[(-12) + (-7h - 12) + (14h - 12) + ..... + (7(n - 1)h - 12)]
= {tex}\mathop {{\text{lim}}}\limits_{{\text{h}} \to {\text{0}}} {\text{h}}{/tex} [7h[1 + 2 + ......+ (n - 1)] - 12n]
= {tex}\mathop {{\text{lim}}}\limits_{{\text{h}} \to {\text{0}}} {\text{h}}{/tex} {tex}\left[7 h \frac{(n-1) n}{2}-.12 n\right]{/tex} 
= {tex}\mathop {{\text{lim}}}\limits_{{\text{h}} \to {\text{0}}} {\text{h}}{/tex} {tex}\left[\frac{7}{2}(n h)(n h-h)-12 n h\right]{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{7}{2}(3)(3-0)-12 \times 3{/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{7 \times 9}{2}-36=\frac{-9}{2}{/tex}

solution_text: We have {tex}I = \int\limits_0^{\frac{\pi }{2}} {\frac{{{{\tan }^7}x}}{{{{\cot }^7}x + {{\tan }^7}x}}dx}{/tex} ...(i) {tex}= \int\limits_0^\pi {\frac{{{{\tan }^7}\left( {\frac{\pi }{2} - x} \right)}}{{{{\cot }^7}\left( {\frac{\pi }{2} - x} \right) + {{\tan }^7}\left( {\frac{\pi }{2} - x} \right)}}dx} \,\,by\,\,\left( {{P_4}} \right){/tex} {tex}= \int\limits_0^\pi {\frac{{{{\cot }^7}\left( x \right)dx}}{{{{\cot }^7}xdx + {{\tan }^7}x}}}{/tex} ...(2) Adding (1) and (2), we get {tex}2I = \int\limits_0^{\frac{\pi }{2}} {\left( {\frac{{{{\tan }^7}x + {{\cot }^7}x}}{{{{\tan }^7}x + {{\cot }^7}x}}} \right)dx}{/tex} {tex} = \int\limits_0^{\frac{\pi }{2}} {dx} {/tex} which gives {tex}I = \frac{\pi }{4}{/tex}
solution_html:
We have

{tex}I = \int\limits_0^{\frac{\pi }{2}} {\frac{{{{\tan }^7}x}}{{{{\cot }^7}x + {{\tan }^7}x}}dx}{/tex} ...(i)

{tex}= \int\limits_0^\pi {\frac{{{{\tan }^7}\left( {\frac{\pi }{2} - x} \right)}}{{{{\cot }^7}\left( {\frac{\pi }{2} - x} \right) + {{\tan }^7}\left( {\frac{\pi }{2} - x} \right)}}dx} \,\,by\,\,\left( {{P_4}} \right){/tex}

{tex}= \int\limits_0^\pi {\frac{{{{\cot }^7}\left( x \right)dx}}{{{{\cot }^7}xdx + {{\tan }^7}x}}}{/tex} ...(2)

Adding (1) and (2), we get

{tex}2I = \int\limits_0^{\frac{\pi }{2}} {\left( {\frac{{{{\tan }^7}x + {{\cot }^7}x}}{{{{\tan }^7}x + {{\cot }^7}x}}} \right)dx}{/tex}

{tex} = \int\limits_0^{\frac{\pi }{2}} {dx} {/tex} which gives {tex}I = \frac{\pi }{4}{/tex}

solution_text: We have I = {tex}\int_{2}^{8} \frac{\sqrt{10-x}}{\sqrt{x}+\sqrt{10-x}} d x{/tex} .......(i) = {tex}\int_{2}^{8} \frac{\sqrt{10-(10-x)}}{\sqrt{10-x}+\sqrt{10-(10-x)}} d x{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} I = {tex}\int_{2}^{8} \frac{\sqrt{x}}{\sqrt{10-x}+\sqrt{x}} d x{/tex} ......(ii) Adding (i) and (ii), we get 2I = {tex}\int_{2}^{8} 1 d x{/tex} = 8 - 2 = 6 Hence I = 3
solution_html:
We have
I = {tex}\int_{2}^{8} \frac{\sqrt{10-x}}{\sqrt{x}+\sqrt{10-x}} d x{/tex} .......(i)
= {tex}\int_{2}^{8} \frac{\sqrt{10-(10-x)}}{\sqrt{10-x}+\sqrt{10-(10-x)}} d x{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} I = {tex}\int_{2}^{8} \frac{\sqrt{x}}{\sqrt{10-x}+\sqrt{x}} d x{/tex} ......(ii)
Adding (i) and (ii), we get
2I = {tex}\int_{2}^{8} 1 d x{/tex} = 8 - 2 = 6
Hence I = 3

solution_text: We have {tex}I = \int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{4}} \sqrt{1+\sin 2 x} d x{/tex} {tex}=\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{4}} \sqrt{(\sin x+\cos x)^{2}} d x{/tex} = {tex}\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{4}}(\sin x+\cos x) d x{/tex} = {tex}[-\cos x+\sin x]_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{4}}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow I = 1.{/tex}

 {tex}I = \int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{4}} \sqrt{1+\sin 2 x} d x{/tex}

solution_text: Let {tex}{x^2} = t{/tex} . Then {tex}\frac{{{x^2}}}{{{x^4} + {x^2} - 2}} = \frac{t}{{{t^2} + t - 2}} = \frac{t}{{\left( {t + 2} \right)\left( {t - 1} \right)}} = \frac{A}{{t + 2}} + \frac{B}{{t - 1}}{/tex} So {tex}t = A\left( {t - 1} \right) + B\left( {t + 2} \right){/tex} Putting t=-2 ,we get A= {tex}\frac{2}{3}{/tex} Putting t=1,we get B= {tex}\frac{1}{3}{/tex} So {tex}\frac{{{x^2}}}{{{x^4} + {x^2} - 2}} = \frac{2}{3}\frac{1}{{{x^2} + 2}} + \frac{1}{3}\frac{1}{{{x^2} - 1}}{/tex} Therefore, {tex}\int {\frac{{{x^2}}}{{{x^4} + {x^2} - 2}}dx = \frac{2}{3}\int {\frac{1}{{{x^2} + 2}}dx} + \frac{1}{3}\int {\frac{{dx}}{{{x^2} - 1}}} } {/tex} {tex} = \frac{2}{3}\frac{1}{{\sqrt 2 }}{\tan ^{ - 1}}\frac{x}{{\sqrt 2 }} + \frac{1}{6}\log \left| {\frac{{x - 1}}{{x + 1}}} \right| + C{/tex}
solution_html:
Let {tex}{x^2} = t{/tex}. Then

{tex}\frac{{{x^2}}}{{{x^4} + {x^2} - 2}} = \frac{t}{{{t^2} + t - 2}} = \frac{t}{{\left( {t + 2} \right)\left( {t - 1} \right)}} = \frac{A}{{t + 2}} + \frac{B}{{t - 1}}{/tex}

So {tex}t = A\left( {t - 1} \right) + B\left( {t + 2} \right){/tex}

Putting t=-2 ,we get A={tex}\frac{2}{3}{/tex}

Putting t=1,we get B={tex}\frac{1}{3}{/tex}

So {tex}\frac{{{x^2}}}{{{x^4} + {x^2} - 2}} = \frac{2}{3}\frac{1}{{{x^2} + 2}} + \frac{1}{3}\frac{1}{{{x^2} - 1}}{/tex}

Therefore,

{tex}\int {\frac{{{x^2}}}{{{x^4} + {x^2} - 2}}dx = \frac{2}{3}\int {\frac{1}{{{x^2} + 2}}dx} + \frac{1}{3}\int {\frac{{dx}}{{{x^2} - 1}}} } {/tex}

{tex} = \frac{2}{3}\frac{1}{{\sqrt 2 }}{\tan ^{ - 1}}\frac{x}{{\sqrt 2 }} + \frac{1}{6}\log \left| {\frac{{x - 1}}{{x + 1}}} \right| + C{/tex}

solution_text: We have I = {tex}\int \frac{x^{3}+x}{x^{4}-9} d x{/tex} = {tex}\int \frac{x^{3}}{x^{4}-9} d x+\frac{x d x}{x^{4}-9}{/tex} = I 1 + I 2 Now I 1 = {tex}\int \frac{x^{3}}{x^{4}-9}{/tex} Put t = x 4 - 9 so that 4x 3 dx = dt. Therefore I 1 = {tex}\frac{1}{4} \int \frac{d t}{t}=\frac{1}{4} \log |t|+\mathrm{C}_{1}=\frac{1}{4} \log \left|x^{4}-9\right|+\mathrm{C}_{1}{/tex} Again, I 2 = {tex}\int \frac{x d x}{x^{4}-9}{/tex} Put x 2 = u so that 2x dx = du. Then I 2 = {tex}\frac{1}{2} \int \frac{d u}{u^{2}-(3)^{2}}=\frac{1}{2 \times 6} \log \left|\frac{u-3}{u+3}\right|+\mathrm{C_2}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{12} \log \left|\frac{x^{2}-3}{x^{2}+3}\right|+C_{2}{/tex} Thus I = I 1 + I 2 = {tex}\frac{1}{4} \log \left|x^{4}-9\right|+\frac{1}{12} \log \left|\frac{x^{2}-3}{x^{2}+3}\right|+C{/tex}
solution_html:
We have
I = {tex}\int \frac{x^{3}+x}{x^{4}-9} d x{/tex} = {tex}\int \frac{x^{3}}{x^{4}-9} d x+\frac{x d x}{x^{4}-9}{/tex} = I1 + I2 
Now I1 = {tex}\int \frac{x^{3}}{x^{4}-9}{/tex} 
Put t = x4 - 9 so that 4x3 dx = dt. Therefore
I1 = {tex}\frac{1}{4} \int \frac{d t}{t}=\frac{1}{4} \log |t|+\mathrm{C}_{1}=\frac{1}{4} \log \left|x^{4}-9\right|+\mathrm{C}_{1}{/tex} 
Again, I2 = {tex}\int \frac{x d x}{x^{4}-9}{/tex} 
Put x2 = u so that 2x dx = du. Then
I2 = {tex}\frac{1}{2} \int \frac{d u}{u^{2}-(3)^{2}}=\frac{1}{2 \times 6} \log \left|\frac{u-3}{u+3}\right|+\mathrm{C_2}{/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{1}{12} \log \left|\frac{x^{2}-3}{x^{2}+3}\right|+C_{2}{/tex} 
Thus I = I1 + I2
= {tex}\frac{1}{4} \log \left|x^{4}-9\right|+\frac{1}{12} \log \left|\frac{x^{2}-3}{x^{2}+3}\right|+C{/tex}

solution_text: According to the question, {tex} I = \int _ \limits{ 0 } ^ {\frac { \pi} 2 } \frac { \sin ^ { 2 } x } { \sin x + \cos x } d x{/tex} ...(i) {tex} \Rightarrow I = \int _\limits { 0 } ^ {\frac { \pi} 2 } \frac { \sin ^ { 2 } \left( \frac { \pi } { 2 } - x ) d x \right]{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow \quad I = \int _ \limits{ 0 } ^ { \frac {\pi} 2 } \frac { \cos ^ { 2 } x } { \cos x + \sin x } d x{/tex} ...(ii) Adding Equations (i) and (ii), {tex}\Rightarrow 2 I = \int _\limits{ 0 } ^ {\frac {\pi} 2 } \frac { \sin ^ { 2 } x + \cos ^ { 2 } x } { \sin x + \cos x } d x{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow \quad 2 I = \int _ \limits{ 0 } ^ {\frac { \pi} 2 } \frac { 1 } { \sin x + \cos x } d x{/tex} [ {tex} \because{/tex} {tex}sin^2 x + cos^2 x = 1{/tex} ] {tex} \Rightarrow 2 I = \int _ \limits{ 0 } ^ {\frac {\pi} 2 } \frac { 1 } { \frac { 2 \tan \frac {x} 2} { 1 + \tan ^ { 2 } \frac {x} 2} + \frac { 1 - \tan ^ { 2 } \frac {x} 2} { 1 + \tan ^ { 2 } \frac {x} 2} } d x{/tex} {tex} \left[ \because \sin x = \frac { 2 \tan \frac { x } { 2 } } { 1 + \tan ^ { 2 } \frac { x } { 2 } } \text { and } \cos x = \frac { 1 - \tan ^ { 2 } \frac { x } { 2 } } { 1 + \tan ^ { 2 } \frac { x } { 2 } } \right]{/tex} {tex} = \int _\limits { 0 } ^ {\frac {\pi} 2 } \frac { \sec ^ { 2 } \frac {x} 2 } { 2 \tan \frac {x }2 + 1 - \tan ^ { 2 } \frac {x} 2 } d x{/tex} Put tan {tex} \frac { x } { 2 } = t \Rightarrow \sec ^ { 2 } \frac { x } { 2 } \cdot \frac { 1 } { 2 } d x = d t \Rightarrow \sec ^ { 2 } \frac { x } { 2 } d x = 2 d t{/tex} Lower limit when x = 0, then t = tan 0 = 0 Upper limit when x = {tex} \frac { \pi } { 2 },{/tex} then t = tan {tex} \frac { \pi } { 4 } = 1.{/tex} {tex} \therefore \quad 2 I = \int _\limits { 0 } ^ { 1 } \frac { 2 d t } { 2 t + 1 - t ^ { 2 } } d t = 2 \int _\limits { 0 } ^ { 1 } \frac { d t } { - \left[ t ^ { 2 } - 2 t - 1 \right] } d t{/tex} {tex} = 2 \int _ \limits{ 0 } ^ { 1 } \frac { d t } { - \left[ ( t - 1 ) ^ { 2 } - 1 - 1 \right] } d t = 2 \int _ \limits{ 0 } ^ { 1 } \frac { d t } { ( \sqrt { 2 } ) ^ { 2 } - ( t - 1 ) ^ { 2 } }{/tex} {tex} = \left[ \frac { 2 } { 2 \sqrt { 2 } } \log \left| \frac { \sqrt { 2 } + t - 1 } { \sqrt { 2 } - t + 1 } \right| \right] _ { 0 } ^ { 1 }{/tex} {tex} \left[ \because \int \frac { d x } { a ^ { 2 } - x ^ { 2 } } = \frac { 1 } { 2 a } \log \left| \frac { a + x } { a - x } \right| + C \right]{/tex} {tex} = \frac { 1 } { \sqrt { 2 } } \left[ \log \frac { \sqrt { 2 } + 1 - 1 } { \sqrt { 2 } - 1 + 1 } - \log \frac { \sqrt { 2 } + 0 - 1 } { \sqrt { 2 } - 0 + 1 } \right]{/tex} {tex} = \frac { 1 } { \sqrt { 2 } } \left[ \log 1 - \log \frac { \sqrt { 2 } - 1 } { \sqrt { 2 } + 1 } \right]{/tex} {tex} = - \frac { 1 } { \sqrt { 2 } } \log \left[ \frac { \sqrt { 2 } - 1 } { \sqrt { 2 } + 1 } \times \frac { \sqrt { 2 } + 1 } { \sqrt { 2 } + 1 } \right]{/tex} [ {tex} \because{/tex} log 1 = 0] {tex} = \frac { - 1 } { \sqrt { 2 } } \log \frac { 2 - 1 } { ( \sqrt { 2 } + 1 ) ^ { 2 } }{/tex} [ {tex} \because{/tex} (a - b) (a + b) = a 2 - b 2 ] {tex} = \frac { - 1 } { \sqrt { 2 } } \log \frac { 1 } { ( \sqrt { 2 } + 1 ) ^ { 2 } }{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow \quad 2 I = \frac { 2 } { \sqrt { 2 } } \log ( \sqrt { 2 } + 1 ) \Rightarrow I = \frac { 1 } { \sqrt { 2 } } \log ( \sqrt { 2 } + 1 ) {/tex}
solution_html:
According to the question,  {tex} I = \int _ \limits{ 0 } ^ {\frac { \pi} 2 } \frac { \sin ^ { 2 } x } { \sin x + \cos x } d x{/tex} ...(i)
{tex} \Rightarrow I = \int _\limits { 0 } ^ {\frac { \pi} 2 } \frac { \sin ^ { 2 } \left( \frac { \pi } { 2 } - x \right) } { \sin \left( \frac { \pi } { 2 } - x \right) + \cos \left( \frac { \pi } { 2 } - x \right) } d x{/tex}
{tex} \left[ \because \int _ \limits{ 0 } ^ { a } f ( x ) d x = \int _ \limits{ 0 } ^ { a } f ( a - x ) d x \right]{/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow \quad I = \int _ \limits{ 0 } ^ { \frac {\pi} 2 } \frac { \cos ^ { 2 } x } { \cos x + \sin x } d x{/tex} ...(ii)
Adding Equations (i) and (ii),
{tex}\Rightarrow 2 I = \int _\limits{ 0 } ^ {\frac {\pi} 2 } \frac { \sin ^ { 2 } x + \cos ^ { 2 } x } { \sin x + \cos x } d x{/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow \quad 2 I = \int _ \limits{ 0 } ^ {\frac { \pi} 2 } \frac { 1 } { \sin x + \cos x } d x{/tex}
[{tex} \because{/tex}{tex}sin^2 x + cos^2 x = 1{/tex}]
{tex} \Rightarrow 2 I = \int _ \limits{ 0 } ^ {\frac {\pi} 2 } \frac { 1 } { \frac { 2 \tan \frac {x} 2} { 1 + \tan ^ { 2 } \frac {x} 2} + \frac { 1 - \tan ^ { 2 } \frac {x} 2} { 1 + \tan ^ { 2 } \frac {x} 2} } d x{/tex}
{tex} \left[ \because \sin x = \frac { 2 \tan \frac { x } { 2 } } { 1 + \tan ^ { 2 } \frac { x } { 2 } } \text { and } \cos x = \frac { 1 - \tan ^ { 2 } \frac { x } { 2 } } { 1 + \tan ^ { 2 } \frac { x } { 2 } } \right]{/tex}
{tex} = \int _\limits { 0 } ^ {\frac {\pi} 2 } \frac { \sec ^ { 2 } \frac {x} 2 } { 2 \tan \frac {x }2 + 1 - \tan ^ { 2 } \frac {x} 2 } d x{/tex}
Put tan {tex} \frac { x } { 2 } = t \Rightarrow \sec ^ { 2 } \frac { x } { 2 } \cdot \frac { 1 } { 2 } d x = d t \Rightarrow \sec ^ { 2 } \frac { x } { 2 } d x = 2 d t{/tex}
Lower limit when x = 0, then t = tan 0 = 0
Upper limit when x = {tex} \frac { \pi } { 2 },{/tex} then t = tan {tex} \frac { \pi } { 4 } = 1.{/tex}
{tex} \therefore \quad 2 I = \int _\limits { 0 } ^ { 1 } \frac { 2 d t } { 2 t + 1 - t ^ { 2 } } d t = 2 \int _\limits { 0 } ^ { 1 } \frac { d t } { - \left[ t ^ { 2 } - 2 t - 1 \right] } d t{/tex}
{tex} = 2 \int _ \limits{ 0 } ^ { 1 } \frac { d t } { - \left[ ( t - 1 ) ^ { 2 } - 1 - 1 \right] } d t = 2 \int _ \limits{ 0 } ^ { 1 } \frac { d t } { ( \sqrt { 2 } ) ^ { 2 } - ( t - 1 ) ^ { 2 } }{/tex}
{tex} = \left[ \frac { 2 } { 2 \sqrt { 2 } } \log \left| \frac { \sqrt { 2 } + t - 1 } { \sqrt { 2 } - t + 1 } \right| \right] _ { 0 } ^ { 1 }{/tex}
{tex} \left[ \because \int \frac { d x } { a ^ { 2 } - x ^ { 2 } } = \frac { 1 } { 2 a } \log \left| \frac { a + x } { a - x } \right| + C \right]{/tex}
{tex} = \frac { 1 } { \sqrt { 2 } } \left[ \log \frac { \sqrt { 2 } + 1 - 1 } { \sqrt { 2 } - 1 + 1 } - \log \frac { \sqrt { 2 } + 0 - 1 } { \sqrt { 2 } - 0 + 1 } \right]{/tex}
{tex} = \frac { 1 } { \sqrt { 2 } } \left[ \log 1 - \log \frac { \sqrt { 2 } - 1 } { \sqrt { 2 } + 1 } \right]{/tex}
{tex} = - \frac { 1 } { \sqrt { 2 } } \log \left[ \frac { \sqrt { 2 } - 1 } { \sqrt { 2 } + 1 } \times \frac { \sqrt { 2 } + 1 } { \sqrt { 2 } + 1 } \right]{/tex} [{tex} \because{/tex}log 1 = 0]
{tex} = \frac { - 1 } { \sqrt { 2 } } \log \frac { 2 - 1 } { ( \sqrt { 2 } + 1 ) ^ { 2 } }{/tex} [{tex} \because{/tex}(a - b) (a  + b) = a2 - b2]
{tex} = \frac { - 1 } { \sqrt { 2 } } \log \frac { 1 } { ( \sqrt { 2 } + 1 ) ^ { 2 } }{/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow \quad 2 I = \frac { 2 } { \sqrt { 2 } } \log ( \sqrt { 2 } + 1 ) \Rightarrow I = \frac { 1 } { \sqrt { 2 } } \log ( \sqrt { 2 } + 1 ) {/tex}

solution_text: Let, I = {tex}\int_{0}^{1} x\left(\tan ^{-1} x\right)^{2} d x{/tex} Integrating by parts, we have I = {tex}\left[\frac{x^{2}}{2}\left(\tan ^{-1} x\right)^{2}\right]_{0}^{1}-\frac{1}{2} \int_{0}^{1} x^{2} \cdot 2 \frac{\tan ^{-1} x}{1+x^{2}} d x{/tex} = {tex}\frac{\pi^{2}}{32}-\int_{0}^{1} \frac{x^{2}}{1+x^{2}} \cdot \tan ^{-1} x d x{/tex} = {tex}\frac{\pi^{2}}{32}-I_1{/tex} , where I 1 = {tex}\int_{0}^{1} \frac{x^{2}}{1+x^{2}} \tan ^{-1} x d x{/tex} Now I 1 = {tex}\int_{0}^{1} \frac{x^{2}+1-1}{1+x^{2}} \tan ^{-1} x d x{/tex} = {tex}\int_{0}^{1} \tan ^{-1} x d x-\int_{0}^{1} \frac{1}{1+x^{2}} \tan ^{-1} x d x{/tex} = I 2 - {tex}\frac{1}{2}\left(\left(\tan ^{-1} x\right)^{2}\right)_{0}^{1}{/tex} = I 2 - {tex}\frac{\pi^{2}}{32}{/tex} Where, I 2 = {tex}\int_{0}^{1} \tan ^{-1} x d x{/tex} = {tex}\left[x \tan ^{-1} x\right]_{0}^{1}-\int_{0}^{1} \frac{x}{1+x^{2}} d x{/tex} = {tex}\frac{\pi}{4}-\frac{1}{2}\left(\log \left|1+x^{2}\right|\right)_{0}^{1}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{\pi}{4}-\frac{1}{2} \log 2{/tex} Thus, I 1 = {tex}\frac{\pi}{4}-\frac{1}{2} \log 2-\frac{\pi^{2}}{32}{/tex} Therefore, I = {tex}\frac{\pi^{2}}{32}-\frac{\pi}{4}+\frac{1}{2} \log 2+\frac{\pi^{2}}{32}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{\pi^{2}}{16}-\frac{\pi}{4}+\frac{1}{2} \log 2{/tex} = {tex}\frac{\pi^{2}-4 \pi}{16}+\log \sqrt{2}{/tex}
solution_html:
Let, I = {tex}\int_{0}^{1} x\left(\tan ^{-1} x\right)^{2} d x{/tex} 
Integrating by parts, we have
I = {tex}\left[\frac{x^{2}}{2}\left(\tan ^{-1} x\right)^{2}\right]_{0}^{1}-\frac{1}{2} \int_{0}^{1} x^{2} \cdot 2 \frac{\tan ^{-1} x}{1+x^{2}} d x{/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{\pi^{2}}{32}-\int_{0}^{1} \frac{x^{2}}{1+x^{2}} \cdot \tan ^{-1} x d x{/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{\pi^{2}}{32}-I_1{/tex}, where I1 = {tex}\int_{0}^{1} \frac{x^{2}}{1+x^{2}} \tan ^{-1} x d x{/tex} 
Now I1 = {tex}\int_{0}^{1} \frac{x^{2}+1-1}{1+x^{2}} \tan ^{-1} x d x{/tex}
= {tex}\int_{0}^{1} \tan ^{-1} x d x-\int_{0}^{1} \frac{1}{1+x^{2}} \tan ^{-1} x d x{/tex} 
= I2 - {tex}\frac{1}{2}\left(\left(\tan ^{-1} x\right)^{2}\right)_{0}^{1}{/tex} = I2 - {tex}\frac{\pi^{2}}{32}{/tex}
Where, I2 = {tex}\int_{0}^{1} \tan ^{-1} x d x{/tex} = {tex}\left[x \tan ^{-1} x\right]_{0}^{1}-\int_{0}^{1} \frac{x}{1+x^{2}} d x{/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{\pi}{4}-\frac{1}{2}\left(\log \left|1+x^{2}\right|\right)_{0}^{1}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{\pi}{4}-\frac{1}{2} \log 2{/tex}
Thus, I1 = {tex}\frac{\pi}{4}-\frac{1}{2} \log 2-\frac{\pi^{2}}{32}{/tex} 
Therefore, I = {tex}\frac{\pi^{2}}{32}-\frac{\pi}{4}+\frac{1}{2} \log 2+\frac{\pi^{2}}{32}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{\pi^{2}}{16}-\frac{\pi}{4}+\frac{1}{2} \log 2{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{\pi^{2}-4 \pi}{16}+\log \sqrt{2}{/tex}

solution_text: We can redefine f as {tex}f\left( x \right) = \left\{ \begin{gathered} 2 - x,\,\,if\,\, - 1 < x \leqslant 0 \hfill \\ x + 2,\,\,if\,\,0 < x \leqslant 1 \hfill \\ 3x,\,\,if\,\,1 < x \leqslant 2 \hfill \\ \end{gathered} \right.{/tex} Therefore, {tex}\int\limits_{ - 1}^2 {f\left( x \right)dx} {/tex} {tex} = \int\limits_{ - 1}^0 {\left( {2 - x} \right)dx + \int\limits_0^1 {\left( {x + 2} \right)dx + \int\limits_1^2 {3xdx\left( {by{P_2}} \right)} } } {/tex} {tex}= {\left( {2x - \frac{{{x^2}}}{2}} \right)^0} + \left( {\frac{{{x^2}}}{2} + 2x} \right)_0^1+\left( {\frac{{3{x^2}}}{2}} \right)_1^2{/tex} {tex}= 0 - \left( { - 2 - \frac{1}{2}} \right) + \left( {\frac{1}{2} + 2} \right) + 3\left( {\frac{4}{3} - \frac{1}{2}} \right){/tex} {tex} = \frac{5}{2} + \frac{5}{2} + \frac{9}{2} = \frac{{19}}{2}{/tex}

Therefore, {tex}\int\limits_{ - 1}^2 {f\left( x \right)dx} {/tex} {tex} = \int\limits_{ - 1}^0 {\left( {2 - x} \right)dx + \int\limits_0^1 {\left( {x + 2} \right)dx + \int\limits_1^2 {3xdx\left( {by{P_2}} \right)} } } {/tex}

{tex}= {\left( {2x - \frac{{{x^2}}}{2}} \right)^0} + \left( {\frac{{{x^2}}}{2} + 2x} \right)_0^1+\left( {\frac{{3{x^2}}}{2}} \right)_1^2{/tex}

{tex}= 0 - \left( { - 2 - \frac{1}{2}} \right) + \left( {\frac{1}{2} + 2} \right) + 3\left( {\frac{4}{3} - \frac{1}{2}} \right){/tex} {tex} = \frac{5}{2} + \frac{5}{2} + \frac{9}{2} = \frac{{19}}{2}{/tex}

question_text: If {tex}\int \frac{3 e^{x}-5 e^{-x}}{4 e^{x}+5 e^{-x}} d x{/tex} {tex}={/tex} {tex}ax+ b\cdot log |4e^x + 5e^{-x} | +C{/tex} , then
question_html: If {tex}\int \frac{3 e^{x}-5 e^{-x}}{4 e^{x}+5 e^{-x}} d x{/tex} {tex}={/tex} {tex}ax+ b\cdot log |4e^x + 5e^{-x} | +C{/tex}, then

options.explanation: On differentiating both sides, we have {tex}\frac{3 e^{x}-5 e^{-x}}{4 e^{x}+5 e^{-x}}=a+b \frac{\left(4 e^{x}-5 e^{-x}\right)}{4 e^{x}+5 e^{-x}}{/tex} giving 3e x - 5e -x = a (4e x + 5e -x ) + b (4e x - 5e -x ) =(4a + 4b)e x + (5a - 5b)e -x Comparing coefficients on both sides, we obtain 3 = 4a + 4b and -5 = 5a - 5b This verifies a = {tex}\frac{-1}{8}{/tex} , b = {tex}\frac{7}{8}{/tex} . Which is the required solution.

question_text: If f and g are continuous functions in [0, 1] satisfying f(x) = f(a - x) and g(x) + g(a - x) = a, then {tex}\int_{0}^{a} f(x) \cdot g(x) d x{/tex} is equal to
question_html: If f and g are continuous functions in [0, 1] satisfying f(x) = f(a - x) and g(x) + g(a - x) = a, then {tex}\int_{0}^{a} f(x) \cdot g(x) d x{/tex} is equal to

options.explanation: Since I = {tex}\int_{0}^{a} f(x) \cdot g(x) d x{/tex} = {tex}\int_{0}^{a} f(a-x) .g(a-x) d x{/tex} {tex}~~~~~~~~...\int_{0}^{a} f(x) d x=\int_{0}^{a} f(a-x) {/tex} = {tex}\int_{0}^{a} f(x)(a-g(x)) d x{/tex} {tex}...using ~~~f(x)=f(a-x) ~~and ~~g(x)+g(a-x)=a{/tex} = {tex}a \int_{0}^{a} f(x) d x-\int_{0}^{a} f(x) \cdot g(x) d x{/tex} = {tex}a \int_{0}^{a} f(x) d x{/tex} - I {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2 I = {tex}a \int_{0}^{a} f(x) d x{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} I = {tex}\frac a2 \int_{0}^{a} f(x) d x{/tex}

question_text: If x = {tex}\int_{0}^{y} \frac{d t}{\sqrt{1+9 t^{2}}}{/tex} and {tex}\frac{d^{2} y}{d x^{2}}{/tex} = ay, then a is equal to
question_html: If x = {tex}\int_{0}^{y} \frac{d t}{\sqrt{1+9 t^{2}}}{/tex} and {tex}\frac{d^{2} y}{d x^{2}}{/tex} = ay, then a is equal to

options.explanation: Since, x = {tex}\int_{0}^{y} \frac{d t}{\sqrt{1+9 t^{2}}} \Rightarrow \frac{d x}{d y}=\frac{1}{\sqrt{1+9 y^{2}}}{/tex} which gives {tex}\frac{d^{2} y}{d x^{2}}=\frac{18 y}{2 \sqrt{1+9 y^{2}}} \cdot \frac{d y}{d x}=9y{/tex}

question_text: If {tex}\int_{0}^{1} \frac{e^{t}}{1+t} d t=a{/tex} , then {tex}\int_{0}^{1} \frac{e^{t}}{(1+t)^{2}} d t{/tex} is equal to
question_html: If {tex}\int_{0}^{1} \frac{e^{t}}{1+t} d t=a{/tex}, then {tex}\int_{0}^{1} \frac{e^{t}}{(1+t)^{2}} d t{/tex} is equal to

options.explanation: Since, I = {tex}\int_{0}^{1} \frac{e^{t}}{1+t} d t{/tex} = {tex}\left|\frac{1}{1+t} e^{t}\right|_{0}^{1}+\int_{0}^{1} \frac{e^{t}}{(1+t)^{2}} d t{/tex} = a (given) Therefore, {tex}\int_{0}^{1} \frac{e^{t}}{(1+t)^{2}}=a-\frac{e}{2}+1{/tex}
